He was pretty tough and made us do everything pretty much ourselves.  I didn't get credit for the course and a bunch of my friends in there barely passed.  Wouldn't recommend him unless you want to just sit in there to laugh.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: First test will make you think the class is gonna be a breeze but by the second test you are curled up on the floor crying wonder what just happened. He is funny and nice but is unclear and relies on textbook provided power points that do not help. It certainly is passable but if you are not incredibly gifted, you better get ready to study.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: COP2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Dr. Frazer is a good guy. he knows his stuff. the problem is for the average individual taking this class its not too unusual to think its easy and then get hit with a bad grade like a truck. the material is pretty difficult and if you dont put in a TON of effort in youll get a wack grade. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED: FIND A STUDY PARTNER!!! be prepared....

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Simple, if he is your only choice, wait another semester.  I took Calculus 3 and Microbiology with his Orgo 1 class. I studied for his class 3 times as much (my girlfriend can tell you) and I got B in Calculus 3, A in Microbiology and C in his class. I also had a 3.93 GPA. TEACH YOURSELF.  Read the book 2 or 3 times, his lectures are the worst ever

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Professor Frazer wasn't as horrible s the comments all say! He wants his class to pass. Practice! He gives 4 test plus a final. lowest test gets dropped and replaced by the final (if higher). He gives homework which can boost your grade if you put in effort. He has an accent but its not to bad. Pay attention, read his slides and you should be fine

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Dr. Frazer is hilarious and very entertaining but when it comes to organic chemistry, you pretty much teach yourself with the book. He also says that the tests have questions directly from the book, well thats not true. More like ACS. first two tests are the easiest, get good grades on those. study hard. curves the overall grade when he has to.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: COP2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Do not take his class. He has a thick accent. He honestly doesn't care. He'll upload assignments without telling you. He thinks everything is easy and won't go into detail. He expects you to know everything. You practically have to teach yourself

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Prof Frazer does a great job at trying to make Orgo interesting. Did not attend too many lectures because they are not helpful, the book is good. He takes approx. 3 weeks to grade exams and the review sheets posted do not always correlate to the material, or they go ahead in chapters that aren't covered on the exam. Overall, average-hard exams.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Frazer is awful, he doesn't communicate effectively at all, and is very inconsistent. Gave out reviews with errors on the answer keys and took forever to respond to emails. He has a very "I don't care" attitude.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: AWFUL prof. Lectures hard to understand & don't cover material on test AT ALL. Tests EXTREMELY difficult, even when studying on own from textbooklectures. Doesnt care for students. First test gives hope, every one after that hard and late grading so before you know it you're failing the class. No EC, lies about curve. Not clear when hwkquiz due.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: COP2040 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: I've taken Chemistry twice and he is the best Chemistry professor I had so far. He is clear on the ideas of chemistry but his test crazy hard, besides that, he is a great professor, willing to help during office hours.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Alright let me start by saying chemistry is not an easy course to begin with. There is a reason why Chem 1a has a NC rating, so remember you have to WORK for your grade. Nonetheless Proffesor Frazer is really a great go with the flow guy. A lot of students can relate to him and his accent is very unique.  Take him, memorize his slides, and the book

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Long story short, self-teaching class. He storms through lectures so fast and skips the important information. Even if you attend his lectures, not much you can learn from them. Tests are hard. Takes him on average 3 weeks to grade the tests. Do not take him if you want to learn something..

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I left the first exam feeling okay about it, maybe a B. The grades for this test were not posted until the day before the second exam (over a month). So by the time I knew how bad I was doing in the class, it was too late because i had to take test 2 the next day. Test 2 grades posted a month later, also. I dropped this class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Professor Frazer is not good. He is not helpful at all and extremely hard to reach. Barely answers emails. I had to teach myself and read the entire book. However, tests are pretty doable if you read the book and re-do the homework at least 3 times for each chapter. Didnt really go to lecture because its pointless.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I ended up having to retake this chemistry class over again, and this semester I had Professor Frazer teach the chemistry class. Needless to say... I may have to retake the class AGAIN!! He doesn't seem to care about teaching. He blows everything off and says it's "stupid" or too easy and doesn't want to teach it to us. BEWARE OF THIS PROFESSOR!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: His accent really makes it hard to understand the lectures, he says hes going to curve and then doesn't, and he doesn't announce assignments and then you just see them online when it's too late. I had a tutor and studied for the tests harder than anything in my life and pulled out a D, ridiculous.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Organic Chemistry, in general, is a tough subject. Frazer adds entertainment value. People who complain generally don't do what is required to be successful in this type of course. Hence, no matter which orgo professor they take at UCF, they will find a reason to complain. Come to class, do organic chemistry everyday, and you will do well.
Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Has a thick accent that is understandable and jokes around a lot.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM240A | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This professor is seriously horrible. He makes Patino seem like a great teacher, even though that guy good isn't either. The lectures aren't even worth going to. He gets off topic and starts talking how he likes his coffee creamed. At first, I thought his accent was cool but soon you realize that it starts to get on your nerves.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: COP1440 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Do not expect to pass just by attending the lectures, you must read the textbook. He posts his power point notes to webcourses. Only assignments are online quizzes which he is usually fair on the amount of time allowed. Tests are relatively easy if you study and do the assignments. Final was based off previous test questions. Has a ?British? accent

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: COP1440 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: I only went to class a few times. I just read the book and took notes. The tests went right along with the book and OWL assignments.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHS1440 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: The bottom line is that this is not an easy course, but he stays true to his syllabus. You cannot expect to earn an A just by attending lectures, as the lecture is only supplementary to the lesson in the book. Don't be scared by other reviews. You can, and will, pass this course if you are willing to put forth effort on your own time!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: COP1440 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This professor is not curteous to his students. He will not explain concepts clearly. He does not tell you when assignments are placed online. This professor goes VERY FAST (like one chapter per lecture fast). You will have to put in an exponential amount of effort to survive this class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not_Sure_Yet | Review: This guy is an absolutely horrible professor. AVOID HIM!! He scribbles on his pp's during lectures, gives extremely hard tests. He is not clear of his expectations.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Dr. Frazer is extremely helpful in office hours, should you choose to go. I got an A in this class through troubleshooting all of my past tests, make sure you take pictures of them and review prior to the final. There is a lot of personal responsibility involved, Dr. Frazer does not play around! Study hard and study early, and you should be fine.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I understand not wanting to be swayed by biased comments, but never have i seen such an accurate page. saying more is almost redundant. i will say that, today i took an exam that had questions based off of material from 4 chapters ahead of what we are currently covering.rushes through lectures*scribbles mechanisms w/ PP. pen tool*unconcerned

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: A list of complaints from students that semester- all true and you can decide if he's worth taking. 1.) Shows up late to every class 2.) Reads directly from PP and doesn't do explanations 3.) Tests don't mirror class info at all 4.) Disorganized and quizzes/hw due can be assigned w.o him telling you 5.) If he's upset he throws a hissy fit in class

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Do NOT take this professor!!! Lectures are irrelevant to the tests. Promises a curve at the end and never comes through.  Not worth your time.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHS1440 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Avoid him if you can. He is a cool and funny guy but it seems like he just doesn't care about teaching. He reads straight off of powerpoint slides. The class was very disorganized(syllabus was wrong). I got an A but it required a lot of studying for tests. Make sure to study with smart friend; I learned so much more from my friends than Frazer.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHS1440 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Way too difficult of a class for a 1000 level. I took 3 years of IB chem and it still wasn't a breeze. He basically just reads the powerpoint. The homework is due randomly so it's easy to miss. He is unorganized and doesn't alert you. However, if you need any help or missed an assignment he is more than willing to help, SO USE IT. Not a bad class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041B | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great professor, hard class but with proper studying it's easily passable. A lot of student responsibility he will not hold your hand, but he gives all the materials you need to pass.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHS1440 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: If you plan on taking this class you honestly need to have either taken AP Chemistry in HS or some type of ADVANCED Chemistry course prior to taking this class. If you took regular Chemistry or Honors, you will struggle immensely and unless you teach yourself everything and read the book cover to cover, you will most likely fail.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: DO NOT TAKE. He is an awful professor, the class should not be this difficult. Focuses on very small concepts on the tests and glazes over the big points he taught which makes the tests awful. Students in my class had to complain to the school and he gave us a curve but he's also rude and late to every class. Trust me do NOT take him.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHS1440 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Useless teacher. He expect people to know chemistry for a introduction to chemistry class. Test were extremely difficult and he would test you on things he did not explain. He would post the quiz two days before they were due and would not inform anyone. DO NOT TAKE HIM!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: DO NOT TAKE! Worst professor I've had at UCF. He moves quickly through difficult material and his tests are extremely hard. I went to every class and numerous SI sessions and still did very poorly.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This class is a basic chemistry class, it should not be this difficult. Prof. Frazer is very smart, and knows what he is talking about. But his tests are ridiculous and confusing. Won't ever be taking him again for a chem class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not_Sure_Yet | Review: AWFUL PROFESSOR. He seems knowledgeable and like a nice guy, but as a teacher, he is not ideal. Tests are very confusing and help is hard to come by. Does not answer emails, is late to class, and rushes through hard material. If you have an option, try not to take this class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM2041 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: HORRIBLE PROFESSOR, DO NOT TAKE!!! Doesn't respond to emails, changes test dates without good notice, doesn't attend office hours, is ~10 minutes late to class, getting a hold of him is impossible, blatantly lies about what's on the exam (will say it's math but the exam is all theory) and does not explain his expectations clearly. DO NOT TAKE.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He's 10+ minutes late everyday and therefore has to rush through the lecture. Nice guy, but seems really disorganized. Won't be taking him for future chem classes.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: DO NOT take him. He doesn't teach! Uses a diff textbook and he expects you to know Orgo as if you are the professor. Uh...I wouldn't be taking the class if I was at a professor level. Assigns exams whenever he wants; no set dates. Can hardly reach him. MUST schedule appt for office hours. Final is the ACS AND a free response section. No curve. -_-

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2110 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great professor! Very knolegable and cares about His students. It's not an easy class; you will have to study hard; great for med school applicants!!! After taking this professor you will do great on chem section: no need to spend $$ for Kaplan review . Highly recommend.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211L | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Met this instructor twice during the semester during random encounters in the lab where he wasn't a part of the class at all. Class proctored entirely by TA (Andrew Turner). Lab reports tedious with time-wasting "literature" assignments. Lab manual full of typos/missing information. Textbook only for report questions, not included in manual.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Dr. Frazer expects you to be a Organic Chemistry genius. He goes extremely fast and does not explain concepts or problems clearly. He will NOT respond to ANY questions through email; if you want help you have to make an appt. for office hours (inflexible). He is knowledgeable about the subject but, he CANNOT teach it to others(No SI for us).

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Frazer is a cool guy and wants you to succeed. He does blow through material so if you can't study on your own or look at material before class, you'll have a hard time. His exams are out of 115 to 125 pts, 90 being an A so it's not impossible. If you sit first row and do well,he'll help you with anything you need. He gave me a LOR for med school.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He is a good teacher, but organic chem2 is a hard subject. You just have to do all the skill builders and problems in the back of the book twice and you should be fine. I received an A

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This class was very tough.  The exams are extremely difficult.  He does drop one exam, so that helps, but it is still hard to do well in this course.  Go to his office hours for help and SI also.  He is nice, but the class is hard.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: AABBOOOA | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: One thing you will learn from Dr. Frazer is HOW NOT TO TEACH. Also don't buy his friendliness. He will NOT help you. You are on your own when you take his class.
Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Excellent Professor. Very willing to assist if you ask. If you put in the effort, your grade will reflect it.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Absolutely terrible! Completely unwilling to help students, it took 3 emails over 4 wks just to see my tests he wouldnt return, tests on material from another book and lectures from copy/pasts ppts from the assigned book so class is quite useless as is the book material, very vague in explanations and allows no time for questions. I got an A  tho

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: BAD CHOICE. Then again, I wouldn't recommend any of the Organic II professors. Orgo professors at UCF fail miserably at presenting the material in a manner conducive to learning. Frazer's biggest downfall is the exams he gives are based on the OLD TEXTBOOK from over a year ago. Exam's will be on unfamiliar territory.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Dr. Frazer was extremely helpful to those who would seek it out. Going to class is a huge priority because he does not post all of his notes online and he tells you exactly what will be on his tests in class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I am in this class now and the first two tests were extremely difficult with alot of questions that were not covered in class. He has an accent but you can still understand him, he has a problem with saying "yea?" every three seconds. Doesn't give any powerpoints either. Go to SI.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: If you want an A, look elsewhere. Shows up to class late half the time. First 2 tests were VERY difficult and random, with things never taught in class. Final is HARD. Last 4 tests were easier. Atleast 15-20 multiple choice questions per test. A couple reactions and mechanisms on most tests. Class is very disorganized. Avoid if you can.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very fair, but at best a mediocre teacher. The subject is hard so naturally people are going to struggle. He gives hints for the material on the test. GO TO CLASS! Do exactly what he says and a A is attainable. Read and practice lots of problems. find patterns, don't just memorize. Best organic prof if you want a good grade.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Please take someone else. I beg of you. you have to teach yourself everything, go to sarc AND si. You won't gain a thing from him.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This class was so simple. Ye, he might have an accent but this is a very easy subject if you actually pay attention. His tests were so straightforward, study your notebook and do practice problems. Not always there for you in his office hours though. A chem class could not be any easier. Lectures are boring but try hard to listen, it helps a lot

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1045 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: AWFUL stay away.not to sound obnoxious, but I'm a perfectionist and I've never failed in any class until this! He's confusing and hard to understand. Your expected to know how to do difficult problems before even starting the chapter and the tests are confusing because the material is not explained well. doing the problems in the book didn't help

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Unavailable to help outside of office hours. Even in office hours, he is not helpful because he works on his own work related to his research. Arrived late to class multiple times by more than 10 minutes. Never have met a professor so unwilling to help students because his interests lie elsewhere with a no care attitude. Do all work and still fail.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: cool guy, not so much as a professor, im a chem major and he really tried to help me pass but, i ended up getting an NC..go to SI! its the only thing that will help you...or just take the placement test and take any 2045 professor they're all better than him

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: he is absolutely horrible. for my class, didn't even give ppts; just breezed through them. take someone else, anyone else.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I really liked him as a person-very cool in office hours-not so much as a professor. I usually just played angry birds on my phone and let the SI leader teach me how to do everything because he was so unclear. You might as well accomidate your schedule around the SI schedule because that is the only reason I got a B in that class
Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2011 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He's a pretty good teacher but horrible when it comes to office hours. He usually shows up late if at all

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Honestly, chem is a difficult subject. Given that, you gotta put in your own effort. Frazer is actually not that bad. You just really need to pay attention. Sit in the front of the class, focus on him, MAKE SURE YOU GO TO SI! I can't tell you how much it helps. He's a pretty funny dude too.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: DO NOT EVEN LAY YOUR CURSOR OVER THAT REGISTER BUTTON FOR THIS CLASS.  Thick accent, reads straight off of power points, and does not care much for students.  There's a reason why this course offers the NC grade.  It's usually offered in hard courses.  Dear UCF, please please hire some new chemistry teacher for basic chem classes such as these.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This teacher is horrible!! You have to teach your self. He has a very thick accent and you can barely understand what he is saying. All he does is read off power point slides, doesn't really teach. Class is very boring. Waste of time and money!!!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Going to class is useless because he reads off of powerpoint slides that you already have. Tests are a joke. No matter how much you study you will fail. If you can teach chemistry to yourself, take this class, but if you need help, RUN LIKE THE WIND!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: It is a very difficult class. I had to study more for this class than any other class I have had before. Class is very boring but if you don't go, you can almost count on failing. He uses MasteringChemistry.com for quizzes and homework. But it is helpful if you have the corresponding notebook for the course (a copy of all of his PPT slides).

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Class is more difficult than it should be. He doesn't seem to really care about his students and is late to class sometimes and half of the time is in a bad mood. I am a Biology major and I got an A in all of the classes I took that semester except in his class i somehow managed a C. I received D's on my tests no matter how much I studied for them.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2010 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Run Run Run Runaway! do not even consider taking this proff i don't know what's his deal because passing a lower level basic chem class should not be this bloody taxing and yet Professor Frazer makes it so.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: It is not impossible to get a good grade, you just have to pay attention (dont let his accent distract you) and do practice problems in your book. His class is boring as hell but his notebook explains everything you need to know. He is an okay teacher but isn't devoted to his students. His misses his appointments w/ students always.He's a weird one

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Sometimes hard to understand but if you keep going to class you can understand what he is trying to say better. Reads off powerpoint. Test are very very hard. I took chm and valencia and got an A and now taking him and got two C on both test and a C is considered a good grade. Ill be happy when its over.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This professor isn't clear and going to class is a waste of time because he basically just reads the powerpoints you already have in his notebook required for the class,honestly you teach yourslef. Good guy but horrible professor.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Easy teacher. Core chemisty concepts. Goes over all examples that will be on test. Went to class like 7 times over semester. Got an A. Only mistakes on tests were stupid ones. Funny guy, he's also from England so has an accent. Still better than Indian or Arab accent though.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2041 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Prof. Frazer presented the class in a steady, digestible fashion. He walked us through the material, we learned new concepts, we applied them through a few exercises per chapter, ...and we got A's. Some of us in the class, that is. Take Frazer if you accept the challenge to simply digest and exercise few new concepts. That is enough said--from an a

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Anyone who's posted that he doesn't know how to teach OBVIOUSLY has not put in to effort to go to his office and get one-on-one help. If u go 2 his office, he's so helpful and down 2 earth. u won't pass if u aren't self-motivated, and yes, many concepts u have 2 learn on ur own by reading and studying--THAT'S COLLEGE PEOPLE. he's not gonna baby you

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: He is a funny guy and very charismatic but as a studen't in his class it is very hard for me.  I am a pre-med major and always did well in chemistry.  In his class however, I am struggling.  The main reason I think is because he teaches at a fast pace and has a UK accent.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Does his best to explain and help you understand in class. If you study, read the book, and do the online stuff class is passable. Test are kinda hard but not to bad if you just study. I failed all the online quizzes but passed all in class test so dont worry uf you dont do well on the online stuff.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Good luck with his class. He isn't very helpful to be honest. His homework is pretty difficult sometimes, he makes it due and random times, and he withholds the correct answers if you get it wrong so you dont even understand HOW you messed up. I would suggest going to another teacher...

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Everyone keeps putting that he's such a bad professor but in reality, HE'S NOT. You need to keep in mind that every class in college, ESPECIALLY the sciences, is primarily self-taught. Yes, you MUST read the book and do practice questions in order to do well. It does take a lot of effort but its not impossible, and he's always willing to help.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Worst teacher of all time. Do not take his class, he does not know how to teach, you are better off reading a chemistry book and teaching yourself without paying for him to just read off of his powerpoint slides that dont help with the information on tests or quizes. I hope he stops trying to teach soon.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: A LOT of self studying. Tried flashcards with his powerpoints, didn't work as affectively. There's a lot of math on his tests and expects you get them done in 1-3 mins. Practice math problems and know terms. If you need help go to tutoring. Look over your tests during office hours. BUG HIM WITH QUESTIONS. Gives HUGE curves on tests. Got a B.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: You need to read the book, do the homework, go to class, ask questions, and work for your grade regardless of who you take. He sends out notes for each chapter, tells you in class what is going to be on the test and is always willing to help you. He's a really nice guy and if you put in the effort, your grade will reflect that.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Don't take him. He doesn't teach he just reads off the powerpoint and when he asks if everyone understands and three people raise their hands he continues..Made chemistry appear 1000 harder than it really is. Aleks (computer work) doesn't help at all for the tests and wastes your time..Get another teacher!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Not the easiest class but the prof tries to help. Notes are power point and tests are a bit tough but the online program helps.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: If there is any way you can take a professor with a better rating,then I would do that. If you have no choice,then be prepared to basically teach yourself. And,if you need help,don't expect a lot out of him. Just make plans to get help elsewhere. I got a decent grade but felt he made it more difficult & time consuming than it had to be.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Chemistry isn't easy, it's not an easy A. You have to go to class and you have to study. A lot of ppl didn't like him because they did bad, but he's really not that bad of a professor. study and do the work- He tells you that from the beginning. He's more gracious, if he sees that you've been trying.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: many people come on here & complain about how horribly they did in class.  Chem isn't easy!  That being said: Professor Frazer is a GREAT professor IF YOU STUDY.  Go over the material before you go to class, have questions ready to ask him, sit in the front, and pay attention and he'll be your best friend.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Don't bother with the broken up chem you'll do more work with him. He is a horrible teacher, and the most disorganized person I've had the misfortune of knowing. He over emphasizes the conceptual aspects of chemistry when in reality this is a basic chem class.
Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: don't even bother going to class, you just end up teaching yourself anyway.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: this professor is SO terrible. doesn't give a crap about his students. wastes class time by going REALLY fast on difficult concepts, and then spends WAY TO MUCH time on the easy stuff. I honestly think he doesn't know how to teach. he knows chemistry in his head, but he can't put it into understandable words. WORST class I've ever had in my life.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This class is only as hard as you make it.  Frazer does see students and have office hours where he is willing to go over tests and answer questions.  An A is definately attainable as long as you study (use the book!).Overall not a bad class or professor.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I did poorly on half of his tests. However, I did work very hard and began studying for tests 2 weeks prior. I ended up doing well on the final and made a B in the class. I read the textbook/practice problems, bought Chem Cliffnotes book, and studied his slides. Overall, his class was aggravating and frustrating most of the time.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Do the 2045 not split chm because if you dont take the secong half,it counts for nothing.I worked very very hard and got a C. He told us on the first day we are expected to teach ourselves and in class he will reinforce what we teach ourselves.i would not reccomend. Everything is self taught!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Same as other reviewers. He reads you the powerpoints in class and that is all doesn't stop if you have a question just assumes everyone understands doesn't even respond to emails if you try to ask a question.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: do not take this teacher! i had to take the no credit because of how poorly i was doing... he reads straight off the powerpoints and his tests do not even follow what he is teaching... he goes through the material too quickly and he said at the beginning of class to not even bother coming to his office hours and to go straight to the TA...
Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM2045 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This professor is the worst, period. DO NOT TAKE HIM!!!!!!! STRIKE, STRIKE, STRIKE!!!!!!!!!!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I would definitely not recommend this professor!!!!! Ive been a straight A/B student and I got a C in this class with struggle. You have to attend class for his notes and he goes super fast that you cant write them down. His tests are extremely hard!!! Do the online homework it will save your grade!!! Dont take himm if you care about your GPA!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I had a really hard time in this class but I won't blame that on the professor. He himself was a great guy and really knows his stuff. Teaches well in my opinion but you definitely have to study on your own and prior to lessons. Tests were not impossible. If you put in the effort you will be ok. NOT a free 'A'

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Pay attention to EXACTLY what he says will be on test b/c he doesn't finish a chapter b4 making test. Extremely helpful during office hrs tho, just make an appointment and don't wait until the last minute. Seems to care about students. Familiarize yourself with material BEFORE lecture, u will make it easier on yourself. I recommend him for CHM2040.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: WORST "professor" EVER!!! He doesn't teach but reads off the powerpoint which he does make. He doesn't seem to care if you pass his care or not. When you schedule an appointment with him he doesn't even bother to show up. Since late September I tried going in to view my tests in November I finally tracked him down. His only advice is to take an NC.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Funny accent? Okay, maybe. Nice guy? Potentially. Good professor? ABSOLUTELY NOT. He is by far, the absolute worst professor I have had at UCF. I am an A student and this class is KILLING me. He does NOTHING but read off of his slides. I am learning nothing other than what I've taught myself and the online hw takes ridiculously long and is useless.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2040 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Keep up with the online hw and start studying at least a week in advance for the tests. Study mainly by doing the problems at the end of the chapter, and reviewing your notes. Frazer can be a bit lazy while teaching, so you may feel like you're teaching yourself at some points, but overall, he does do a decent job.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Organic II is a difficult class. It was even harder Summer 2009 because it was at 8AM. Frazer helped A LOT because he made the class laugh.His exams were rather difficult towards the end, but it's because you had to know your mechanisms and how to get to the product quickly! Review your exam in his office.. He is an awesome guy to hang out with!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This class is a little hard. If you don't have prior studying about chemistry or have never taken a chemistry course before, it will be harder. I pulled off an A, but I took chemistry in high school, it was still hard. Reading the book is a must. Make sure to go see him about your tests, sometimes he'll give extra points.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: i recommend you take this class if you are looking to drop you GPA. I'm pretty sure he is there because he was given a grant to do research and his intrest in teaching is...nonexistent. Also when I set up an appointment for office hours he didn't show. Don't take this class period.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: ORGO1 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Do the work, read the book and do some of the problems in the back.  You will do fine.  I made an A in the course is it is possible.  This is a real college course, and it is hard.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: In all honesty, he may not be the perfect professor, but he does inform you what each test is on and has accent during class to try and keep your attention. Mastering chemistry and studying is a much. His tests arent impossible, but they arent going to be super easy either. An A is achievable, but most im sure will settle for the B.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: For a 1000 level class he makes this way more difficult than it needs to be. Be prepared to teach yourself from Mastering Chem and the book, because he certainly isn't going to be teaching you anything.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM1032 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: His accent is tough to understand and sounds muffled through the microphone.  He moves too slow on the easy subjects and to fast on the hard ones and the exams dont cover the content well.  He has a bad habit of letting class out early.  I don't recommend this prof.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: great teacher but organic chem is hard no matter what! def attend class and read the book. accent is distracting at first but you get used to it. DON'T YAWN in class!!! or he will point you out and yell at you (jokingly of course)

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I am taking him, because his class was the last one still open. He's a pain, and doesn't care what you don't know. His accent and constant "yah" gets in the way. His tests are hard, and I would never ever ever recommend him to anyone. There are just some teachers that should be replaced.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: OK, he is hard to understand but if you're up front you can get somewhat of what he is saying.  Tests are a piece of cake.  You HAVE to teach yourself by reading the text book and practicing problems on your own.  If you need this class I wouldn't worry. I'm a straight A student and continue to be just that.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: WORST PROFESSOR EVER!!!!! He doesn't even teach, he just talks! Everything is off the powerpoints from masteringchem, but he puts his name on them! Gets angry really easy, but some days he's in the best mood ever. Gave 2 quizzes the day before the final with a total of 175 questions. I highly recommend NOT taking him!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Hard teacher and very difficult to understand because of his accent. Every other sentence you will hear in his lectures all semester is "Got tha ideaaa?" (but no one really gets the idea). Anyways if you take his class you will see what I'm talking about. He also likes to herd birds into his classroom.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This class was a complete nightmare! Has no idea what he's talking about, just reads off the ppt. No set dates for tests,always have errors on them. Doesn't return emails or answer questions, not helpful whatsoever. Gave work on mastering chem day b4 final!!!! If you have to take this class just read the book and teach yourself, thats what I did.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: i swear this guy is like the teacher from the depths of hell but on this earth to fail anyone who has to take chemistry! He sucks! u can't understand him he doesn't even try to clarify himself. Office hours are joke and to put the icing on the cake he insults your intelligence when you ask him a question that he thinks is easy!! don't take him!!!!!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I do not recommend taking this teacher. He means well and he tries to make class more interesting, but he has a bad habit of going off on tangents or going too slow on the easy material and leaving the hard material for students on their own. His tests are challenging but doable if you study but most of the time there are many errors on the exams.
Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Don't like the fact that he doesn't explain anything and glosses over so much.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHEM1032 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I don't recommend this guy if you want to really learn or if you ever need help with things.  He doesn't return e-mails.  The material the class covers is easy yet the tests are impossibly hard and do not reflect the information that is taught.  If I had it to do over I would not take this professor.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1032 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: +1- WORST TEACHER! Do not take this class!! He has an incomprehensiable accent that makes it impossible to understand him. He is full of smart comments and rude things to say. He goes off on tangents every 5 minutes, so he waistes class time. And his tests are nothing like the practice tests! Just trust me avoid taking this guy at all costs.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Absolute worst professor I've ever had. Refused to put test dates on syllabus and only gave couple days notice before test & half the time would change date again. Nothing from review was on test. Summarized what to know for test and test would be completely different. Never returned calls or emails. Spent 1/2 of class time talking about sports.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2210 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very charismatic and likeable man.  When he wants to be he can be a great teacher, but it seems that it's a bother for him to be teaching.  I think his students' biggest problem with him is that he took, on average, over two weeks to grade exams.  Could really be a great teacher if he wanted to be.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Class is kinda hard. He gives a packet with notes but you need to go to class because he elaborates on them. You prob. won't be able to understand the class without the book. Read the chapters and go to the classes and you should be fine. He gives test reviews but NOTHING ON THE REVIEW IS ACTUALLY ON THE TEST.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: hard to understand bc of his accent. makes jokes to keep students paying attention. he hands out note packets so that helped, but he develops more on the notes so you still have to go to class. i never really used the book. tests were difficult. but if your taking chm 1020 you dont have a choice anyways so good luck! (i got a B)

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: i do not recommend taking him. his class is extremely boring and he is hard to understand. the good thing is he gives you a packet of his slides to follow along with and a practice test before the real test but it didnt help at all. and dont plan on asking any questions because you won't get an answer. test are extremely HARD!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He's hard to understand. Doesn't cover what is on the tests. He tries his hardest to avoid answering any questions. Take someone else unless you want to teach yourself and like staying up until 4 am studying for stuff you don't understand.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He's way goofy but that gets him off track and then you dont learn the stuff you need to know. Then the test comes and it's like "where did all this come from?" Its stuff he's never covered. Take someone else

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Not a very goood teacher. At first it is very hard to comprehend what he is saying because of his accent. But with time it gets easy...He is pretty funny. But waste to much time talking about stuff that has nothing to do with what you NEEED to know for the test. I wouldn't recommend this class he really doesn't like to answer questions

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: WORST TEACHER! Do not take this class!! He has an incomprehensiable accent that makes it impossible to understand him. He is full of smart comments and rude things to say. He goes off on tangents every 5 minutes, so he waistes class time. And his tests are nothing like the practice tests! Just trust me avoid taking this guy at all costs.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I would recommend another professor.  The first two tests were easy. The first one i didn't study for and got an A. The second one I read the three chapters the night before and got an A. On the third one i read the chapters and got a D... And the final was not easy. I would recommend another teacher, although he is funny at times, tests are hard

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This class was sooo hard...almost impossible! He doesnt understand that this is a chem class for non science majors. He is funny...i guess but not only does he have an accent, he is a soft speaker. I studied for the tests 2 weeks in advance, read and took notes on all the chapters and still ended up with low C's and D's. Final was rediculous!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Prof. Frazer was very difficult. I'm a decent student and I failed the first test. The test was extremely difficult, didn't really follow the study guide, and the way the questions were worded was very tricky, almost like he was trying to get people to put the wrong answer. I would deffinately advise anyone to pick another proffessor!!!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Do not be fooled by the easy concepts being taught in class. His lectures/notes and the book do not match up to the difficulty of his tests. They are very applied questions so you must understand the material very well. He doesn't get this is chemistry for liberal arts majors!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: The guy lectured all day about the notes he gave us.  The course concepts themselves were easy, but the tests he gave us were poorly worded.  He can be funny when you can understand him.  Do yourself a favor and teach yourself instead of going to class, only attend on test day and show up to grab the notes and study guide...then leave.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2010 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: hard tests. after the first test i thought id get a A in the class..i ended up getting a C in the class. dont bother going to class.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I was in Chemistry Honors in High School and had a tough teacher. I made A's and B'. Prof. Frazer however, has a British accent which isn't too hard to understand and the level he teaches on makes it easy to understand basic Chemistry, but the tests are ridiculously difficult and you MUST study! I do not recommend taking this class. Study to get C.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CMH2020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Prof. Frazier is the MAN! His Brittish accent adds to the fun! He tells really neat stories and will actually interact with people in the first few rows. He will make comments about people who yawn, or cough, or sneeze, but he is only joking around. I'm not a science type person, but Prof. Frazier makes it fun!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: His accent makes it a little hard to understand him. He is a really nice and funny guy though. Tests are ok. You must study notes and reading the book helps but its not mandatory

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He can b funny sometimes, but hes really hard to follow.  To do well in the class you really need to read the book, and go to every class(even though most of the class is a waste). the subjects he covers are simple, but it takes a long time for him to explain it, and it usually confuses you more. I ended up teaching myself most of what we covered.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I sat in the front row, read ahead a chapter before each class, and I got an A on the tests. If you sit in the front, he'll talk TO you and it's easier to pay attention and retain what he's saying. He also will help with test answers in his office hours. You have to read the book if you want an A and come to class, but he gives you the notes. A+++!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2010 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: His classes were not too boring and he is a funny guy but when it comes to helping you out, that's not his specialty. Read the chapters and some of the test questions come from the end chapter assessments. His tests are hard if you are not a chem person though and he doesn't really curve

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I wonder why no one failed to mention his terrible accent. It's hard to understand him. He doesnt like when people sneeze in his class, coughs, or asks questions. Unfortunately, hes the only teacher that teaches chem. His tests are definitely hard and his lectures are INSANELY BORING. Not recommended.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: ok. lets have a chat. you HAVE to read your book and bring it with you to class everyday. dont ask questions in class, he wont answer you anyway. You will leave class feeling like you know the concepts, but then the tests are hard. he is an entertaining guy, sometimes he gets off subject. attendance is not mandatory, but if you want to pass...go.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Got an A with a little bit of effort. He really hates stupid people, and it gets pretty hilarious because people ask some really idiotic questions (usually about something he JUST talked about). So if you have the capacity to listen and read you should be fine. He doesn't hold hands, we're talking about college here. Your grades are your own doing. He IS helpful if you ask pertinent questions. I also had to call him at 8pm for something and he was very friendly and helped me out, no problem.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Funny guy. His tests are not easy, but by attending class and reading the book I managed to get As on all of them. Kids in class asked a lot of redundant questions and he doesn't stand for it. Would prefer people LISTEN or read the book. Great if you can handle science, but if you're taking CHM1020 don't have a choice, he's the only professor.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: i read the textbook, watched the DVD, studied my notes, nothings helped his test are impossible and so is he, he wont help you in any way ...with your grade or any question.. only study the studyguides he gives you

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2111 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Horrible prof. Had him for lab, and didn't teach you a thing. students dreaded him when he came in to "chat" everything was simple to him, and he would mention a little bit of history and where the chemicals came from, a few reaction mech. but when it came to the quiz nothing he taught was on it.  Horrible professor!!!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Andy frazer is the man! He always keeps it real. Talk to him, he's funny as hell. He lectures for only like 5 minutes so you ALWAYS get out of ochem lab earlier than the other sections, ALWAYS. Take him for CHM 2211L, you won't regret it.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Terrible teacher. the only reason I ended up getting a "C" as my final grade is because he probably knew that if he didn't curve grades more than half the class would have failed becuase of the way he teaches. Whatever you do, pick a different Chem teacher, his class is not worth your time!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I love prof. Frazer as a person and he tries to explain but his tests are difficult.  Towards the end he gave practice tests which helped a lot, and as a non chem major, he curves your grade.  He's very funny and entertaining, obviously chem is not enjoyable but he tries.

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM211L | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: really depends on your TA.....but you gotta love Frazar. VERY entertaining..i would suspect more so than any other professor.....and with such a pointless class, entertainment helps....OKIE DOKES?

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM1020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: RUN!!! he seems nice and while in class you think you understand whats goin on and then you get his quizes and tests! theyre not that hard, but the questions are very confusing!the class argues with each other and him on almost every question. people in this class are not planning to continue in chm and he doesnt understand that!!!

Professor: Andrew Frazer | Course: CHM2211 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: He really knows the material but doesn't know how to convery it to the class. He barely lectures the Lab (CHM2211L) the TA does most of the work. He's not even around to help. He shows up during the lap for 5 minutes before the experiement and 5 minutes during. He needs to let go of his ego too. Hard grader, or does he grade? TA does it all!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Class is hard, and professor has a very rude and condescending attitude at times, if you study on your own youll do fine, this class is a weed out class so get ready to study 15-20 hours a week to fully understand topics.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very good professor!! Very fair with grading. His lectures are amazing and worth going to his office hours too!!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Quizzes are extremely difficult for no reason.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took his CS1 class as a CS minor, Mechanical Engineering major. Guha does a great job at making you think hard in every problem, doesn't make anything easy, but doesn't throw any major curveballs either. Use his online notes and office hours and really make an effort to do the programming assignments, and he'll be the best teacher you can have.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: His lectures weren't too bad but you have to understand how coding in the language works to stay up with him. His grading system is up to his discretion based on the averages of his students.. His test are graded hard as evidence by the grade you can have a low grade and still pass. He also talks fluently and very understandable.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: Pointless lectures and made everything difficult for no reason. Bad at teaching.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4516 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Rather not say | Review: #Phenomenal!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great lecturer

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Guha often gives test questions that aren't really covered in class. Tests are hard to predict, despite seeing examples. Homework is graded tough. He disrespected me a few times. One time I asked a question, only to be told to look it up in a dictionary. I reached out after the course for something, only to be answered initially, and the ignored.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Consisted of non-mandatory(you will fail if you dont attend) lectures w/ a quiz or assignment at end of week. Assignments are straightforward , grading was fairly lenient with a curve being applied throughout the course. Expect quizzes to be highly difficult w/ averages often sitting at a C, despite this with curving these often turned into B's.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: The class was really hard and the exams were brutal, but I do think he really cares about students and wants you to really learn C deeply. He gave out a lot of extra credit and you should definitely do all of it. He also curves like crazy. I wouldn't recommend taking him if you aren't a CS major, though.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great professor! Though exams were tough (with the class average usually being around 50%), assignments and lab grades helped boost my grade. The professor does offer extra credit opportunities so pay attention. He does not like to repeat himself about it. Make some friends and don't procrastinate. An A is very doable. You will learn a lot.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Gives little extra credit on assignments and exams. Study early for exams at least 2 weeks. Take his advice of studying 12+ hours and writing 3 practice programs weekly. If you're stuck on a concept, utilize his office hours as he explains them well. Be sure to understand programming assignments. Ask good questions in lecture. Helpful LA sessions.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: The course content is not bad. This professor will teach you 3 things about each 10 subjects and then test you on seven of those topics but will not even hint as to what he will test on (aka you must know everything). Doesn't really do partial credit. KEEP YOUR QUIZZES (extra credit)! Will be helpful to create study group.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Good professor, but "Cryptography" was probably the hardest class I've taken at UCF. I understand that he was trying to challenge us, but sometimes it just felt unnecessary, especially to anyone who has a life or a job. He also likes to throw curveball questions on quizzes/tests. But hey, he curved the class, and I got a B, so I can't complain.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Pros: His lectures are decent. He does a good job makes the lectures engaging and interesting.

Cons: Grading scale is weird. He decides it at the end based on the class average. Tests are often more difficult than needed. Average on the final was 46. He often throws “curveballs” into his tests. Half of the final had these unexpected questions.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: This guy absolutely sucks. Why does he have 100+, 5 rating? This guy makes an elective harder than CS1. The TAs in this class were horrendous, especially this Ian Binstock kid. Saw the kid finish grading each exam in like 10 seconds.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: For someone who emphasizes the attendance being non-mandatory, he sure does incentivize showing up. Arbitrarily gave everyone that came in to pick up their quizzes extra points after he realized the average for his class was a 41.

I passed his class but he sure didn't let it easy on the masses. Class discord complained about him nonstop… Good luck

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: This guy is good at teaching, but the course material in COT3100 is hard. DON'T skip classes just because there's notes online, you will not understand it nearly half as well as if you just went to the lecture and paid attention. People will complain about the class being hard and those are the guys who didn't try to learn at all.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: I think his style of teaching doesn't really fit a begginer course like Intro to C. He's caring but he will give you a tough time. If you're a beginner in coding, I would suggest a different professor but if you're very studious and look for a challenge, you can take this class.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: He's a non-factor, and I refuse to waste another breath on him. Middle finger all the way!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C- | Review: Professor Guhas grading is horrendous. If he suspects you of using ai for anything at all, even if you clearly didn't and you plead your case you will get a zero on that assignment. If you write out a 3-4 paragraph email explaining how and why your work was done by you he will just say he doesn't believe you and leave it at that.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CAP5937 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Strict grader & non-reasonable
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Terrible system. Take someone else.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He's cool when it comes to gradings and homeworks. His grading system is a little weird (he decides what's considered an A, B, etc at the end of the semester), but its beneficial. Main problem: quizzes and tests. He writes them the DAY before and they make little to no sense. Its like trying to decipher Shakespeare and then code. Its ridiculous.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Best Professor of my 4 years of studying for mechanical engineering. I took this class as an elective and it was insanely hard but I learned more than I have in any of my classes by far. I am considering systems simulations masters due to how well he taught this course and how much I learned.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4516 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: One of my favorite classes! Great for practicing coding and algorithms. Can be a bit challenging given the time constraints of the weekly "quizzes" (i.e. competitions), but you can get 80% partial credit over the weekend for any missed questions, which is pretty generous. Heavily curved, and passing is virtually guaranteed if you put in the effort.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Guha is the GPA reaper and his scythe is discrete math.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Difficult class. HW is time consuming and exams are hard. Quizzes are fair. Required much self study and DEEP comprehension. Must take advantage of all the past materials posted to get a good feel of what questions will be asked (although different every time). Guha has good lectures but it can be hard to follow along. Look ahead on the content.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Definitely a good person and a knowledgeable teacher that has a passion for it. However, incredibly advanced work asked of you. Homework is harder than the lecture, and tests are even harder than the homework. For perspective: a C is currently set at a 45%.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Tough. Engaging beyond 110%. Strict but fair professor. Not for the light-hearted. Peak university level class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: The course grade is exam heavy and he puts hard things he never even taught or mentioned in the class on his exams and homework just because he thinks they are "fun" or "cool" problems. I got my AA degree and had a 4.0 gpa, so I know what I'm doing. But now there goes me gpa. Thanks Guha for messing that up for me. Do not take if you like your gpa.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Amazing professor who has passion about what he's teaching. Resources are all available online, including past exams. His lectures do move quickly, but given the amount of topics to cover, the pace is fair. Assignments are good and engaging, but most weight goes to tests. Tests require a lot of creativity & understanding of topics. You will learn!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: He is an amazing teacher and good grades will be achieved as long as you pay attention in class and attend lectures. Though there are many tests and lectures, he really makes sure that you understand the topic and he gives good feedback. He is also easily accessible if help is needed.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I'm still in this class, but from the first couple of weeks I can already tell this isn't going well The grade is extremely test-heavy Lecture attendance isn't required, but if you miss one day you better spend an entire day reviewing notes or watching youtube Recitation is required attendance but it doesn't even cover the same material as in class

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I recommend Professor Guha to high-level students. He is very knowledgeable, so he covers content quickly. This makes him more difficult, but it can be worthwhile because students will learn more. There are two types of exam problems: some are straightforward, and others require intuition and a creative step.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Guha is an excellent communicator and super smart guy. However, his lectures move quickly and can give the impression that students are simply expected to know what he's explaining already. Not an easy class by any means. Tough homework & exams. Two programming projects. Labs are mandatory & graded. Guha is a great person to talk to outside class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B- | Review: The man the myth, Took him for CS2 & Discrete. I would say the best thing about Guha is his webpage, he has previous tests & notes there etc. His tests & assignments are notoriously hard but you will learn from them. Lectures are ok, I didn't get much out of them. You will have to take his test for the FE so he is inevitable.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B | Review: whoa. my worst semester ever, this was absolutely insane. almost lost my mind

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Guha is one of the best CS professors you can have. It's clear he has an immense passion for math, which makes his lectures very engaging. His TA's office hours are very helpful and gives credit for them. He assigns one homework per week (not hard if you go to lectures). His exams are not that hard, just go over previous exams and you'll be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Dr. Guha was an alright lecturer. It was hard to pay attention because the lectures are boring and his way of taking notes is quite terrible (he just writes on paper and uploads it online in a non-visual way). Be prepared that his class is very lecture heavy and his TAs are VERY laid back in grading and knowing the concepts. However, Guha is caring

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: This class was unnecessarily difficult. Would cover topics and examples too fast, and not explain much. Looking back at his old coursework and tests was the best thing for this class, for homework and tests. Be prepared to spend hours trying to teach yourself things you should have learned in class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: You can tell how much Guha loves math / CS, and he really tries to relay that to his students. I think he's a great professor overall - very intelligent, clear course outline, great at teaching. Only downside is there's a ton of content to cover, so his lectures are VERY fast-paced, but he'll almost always stop and answer questions that arise.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: He's a teacher who cares about the content he is teaching, which means he makes it difficult on purpose. Little homework. For some reason, he has projects that involves code and grades it based off that. If you like the subject, take him, if you don't care, don't bother. He's not worth the effort if you don't like this type of math.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Self studying is your best friend, his lectures aren't good. the notes he writes in class are messy and frustrating to follow along with. if you aren't going to attend class, be there to pick up exams bc he might do surprise extra credit. pretty difficult class overall. avoid unless you are doing SCAN minor.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A- | Review: DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR IF YOU CARE ABOUT YOUR GPA. Tests and homework are very difficult. Includes random "cool" things on tests that he briefly mentions in class, with no indication that that material will be on the test. "Hints" to things that might be on the test that he's not going to teach and expects you to teach yourself.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: You'll have to teach yourself everything. The only way to know what's going to be on the test is by finding his previous exams. Rarely responds to emails. Take anyone else for discrete.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Rather not say | Review: At least the good thing about this class is that you learn to enjoy the simple things in life.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very clear on policies and resources. Occasionally references a textbook but the textbook is purely supplementary and online. He gives us access to all his past class resources: quizzes/homeworks/lectures/notes and even the class grade bands and grade spread. Hard material, but will answer all questions thoroughly. Lenient with certain deadlines.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He goes through the material very quickly and expects you to already understand the topics. Gets irritated when you ask questions because in his mind you should understand everything. You will be teaching yourself the material, notes barely help. Letter grade is based on the standard deviation of the class. The average exam grade is a 45-50%

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Rather not say | Review: Expects that you already know the material and doesn't go into depth with some topics. The labs / TAs aren't a big help either. You have to teach yourself or go to the SI or SARC sessions. He teaches pretty fast, so if you don't understand the material to start, take another professor.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Rather not say | Review: The legendary Guha. He has lived up to his reputation. I did not enjoy my time in this class. Content was difficult and the pace was too fast. Recitations were not helpful as TAs just give you problems to solve instead of teaching you. Do not take this class with this professor. Great guy, not teacher.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Made my life a living hell. DO NOT TAKE HIM!!!!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I'm sure Guha is a great professor for other classes but he is very unforgiving, snarky to his students, and he expects students to already intuitively understand the material. Lectures feel more like a showcase of his knowledge than actually learning the material, and his notes are often not helpful. I'd recommend a different professor.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Guha teaches very fast and often assumes that everybody understands the material. Is condescending anytime someone asks a question about something they don't quite understand. Don't expect any help (or sympathy). Be ready to self study! Anyway try to get another prof, but if you can't its not the end of the world.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Had Guha back in the Fall 2023 semester and just didn't get around to rating him till now. I learned a lot in his class and I was super prepared for the FE. However the process of doing so was stressful, way more stressful and unnecessarily competitive had I taken a different professor. Many friends got Bs that could've been As with other profs.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Great professor, super smart. If you want to make sure you learn the material and pass the Foundations Exam, take him. He gives very detailed lectures and is open to answering your questions. Don't take his class with other hard classes. Only issue I had was with the TA. He decides grade cutoffs at the end of the semester but they are quite fair.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review:

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B | Review: This man has a MASSIVE ego and this was seen in his office hours. This class made me question if I chose the right major (computer engineering) and I do not recommend this guy to anyone. If you want to learn the same material in a more fair environment, take someone else. Enough said.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A- | Review: They all say, "if you want to learn, take Guha regardless of the grade you get". At the end of the day, grad school admissions and employers see the grade you got in the class - not how much u learned in class. It was horrible not knowing what your letter grade was and everyone praying on their classmates' downfall due to this man's grading system.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Great professor overall: if you want to learn take this class, enough said.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Builds on skills from COP 3503. Homework is much easier than COP 3502, however, tests seem much more difficult. As long as you cover material from class you should easily be able to get a B. Attend lecture as he might hint what things will be on the exam.
