Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Really nice teacher.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very nice teacher.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: I've heard a lot of good things about Arup and I was not at all expecting anything to be true, but he really is one of the best teachers in CS (I think the best). He isn't easy but it doesn't matter because he makes you want to learn and do well. Every class is interesting, though I may be biased because I actually liked discrete one.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Took honors intro to c class. If you don't know anything about C coming in, you will get lost at some point. He teaches new concepts very quickly. Nice funny guy but be prepared to work your ass off for an A. Tests are open book, helps very little

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Fantastic professor. Challenging, you have to go to class and pay attention to master all the content, but quizzes the first few weeks ensure students keep up at the beginning. Six assignments (could do community service rather than the last) that were extremely challenging, but satisfying to complete. Curved the entire class at the end.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He is hard to follow when introducing new concepts.The speed when introducing new material is way to fast. Since he does not follow the book, its pretty much useless to waste money on it. If you don't have a laptop to take this class, don't take it as its nearly impossible to take notes by hand since this is all coding.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Once again Arup is a great CHALLENGING teacher but you definitely learn something which is what i like about him. Assignments were with a partner. Test 2 was the hardest. Curved class-85 was an A! Had extra credit opportunities. He did the comm. service thing again which was good to avoid an extra assign. learn about history of cryptography in here

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: gotta love arup! hes great. i will take any classes he teaches. very clear on what he wants. his exams were half multiple choice and half frq (open book), easy if you did his review. his programs were challenging but really helped learned the material. if you go to class, you will get a good grade. if not, you'll do fine if u do the programs right.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Excellent teacher.  Really cares about his students and wants them to do well.  You will need to study hard for his exams.  As long as you do more programming than the homework you should be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I've had Mr. Arup Guha (do not get mixed up with Dr. Ratan Guha) for 3 classes now (COP 3223 and COP 3502) and he is by far my favorite professor ever. His classes are challenging but they are very fair. For instance the A in this class was an 85 or above. I suggest going to class he discuss quite a bit of information. Highly recommended.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I'm an EE major and came in with no programming experience. I had no problem with the course. It was a lot of fun, actually. Mr. Guha makes the material interesting, gives it to you in easy-to-manage chunks, and is always clear. I walked out of his lectures ready to apply what I'd just learned, and I even found myself programming for enjoyment.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: There is a saying about this teacher.  "If you are a CS major, you will appreciate him. If not, you won't."  He expects a lot of you, you need to work hard, but if you are interested in computer science, he will get you to where you need to be.  Great professor.  He cares a lot, and his grading is fair, despite how hard his tests are.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Okay guy. Grading is weird.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great professor. I didnt have any programming experience but passed with an A. Provides clear examples in class and plenty of practice programs online. Dont be stupid tho, he challenges you with his tests so dont assume its enough to just go to class. pay attention in class, take time on the hw, and review concepts by messing with some programs.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup is the best professor I've been taught by so far. He makes his tests challenging to ensure that students study, but he has an extremely fair curving system. If you're struggling, he will go out of his way to break down something step by step. Definitely looking to take more classes with him. TAKE HIM!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This class was SOOOO hard for me.  I tried very hard, and thankfully I passed.  Others had no problem, but I am sure they had more programming experience than I did.  The tests are majorly curved.  He is super nice and helpful. Make sure you visit his office reg.  if you are in one of his classes.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is without a doubt the most interested Professor I've had. He genuinely cares about his students and his interest in the material is infectious. It's not an easy class, but that's not his doing - it's tough material. Curves generously, but not so much so that you can't try. No textbook use. Take Arup, it's really that simple.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Arup is one of the best teachers at UCF. His homework and tests are difficult because he forces you to think in depth about the material he goes over.  I got a B in the class and I wouldn't consider myself an efficient programmer.  This class does require hard work, but you will learn.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Arup is one of the best professors I have come across in my three years of college! He is clear and goes at a great pace. You MUST go to class though. It's not that bad, I'm sure you can endure it. Go to TAs for help on assignments.  Grading is very fair!! He sets his own grade lines depending on the class's performance. Go to cs.ucf.edu/~dmarino. You'll find past grades, comments from students and everything you want to know about arup! I definitely recommend this professor! Take him!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He is a great lecturer and gives hard homework, but you are guaranteed to learn in his class. He is extremely clear in explanations.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: If you have experience with C (or other programming) this class will probably be easy. If you haven't had any it can be very tough at times. His tests are tricky, get use to the way he likes to ask output and tracing questions before your first test. Programs are easy in the beginning than get difficult, and take alot of time to write.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Horrible Teacher. He is easy if you know programming before hand, but if you don't you will struggle. Tests are very tricky. He loves to put tricks in his questions. Programs are alright but get hard near the end. Overall, I recommend not taking him if you are new to C

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He is very helpful. Maybe a little too helpful. He will sometimes come late to class because he was helping students in his class. He does not show up to every class mainly because he is sooooooo busy. I never used his book although his test used open books. I still ended up with an A. Just attend class and he spoon feeds you the material.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Lecturs are very personal, this guy isn't a concrete face, actually has personality. Don't need the book or really need to attend lectures, class was pretty easy. If you've already taken upper division math courses and have an engineering/mathematics mindset, you'll do fine. If you're a retarded business or IT major then you'll suck.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I've had Guha for 4 different classes now and his methods go across all of them. He's not easy, by any means, but he is the first professor at UCF I've felt TRULY cared about his students. He's brilliant, very personable, and is just an all around good guy. I don't care what grade you get, you'll walk out of his class having learned something.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: This guy is the man!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This class was hard for me. But keep in mind I had no previous programming experience and very little interest(took it b/c I had to) compared to other prof he's prob the best teacher, but he's test are Hard. 2test plus final and 6 programming assignments. Dont buy the book, everything for his class is on line.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Awesome Professor!! He is extermely clear and he simplifies EVERYTHING so its very understandable. Is funny at times, but always cool, and never loses focus off teaching. Definetly take him over any other professor for ANY computer science class. Tests are kida hard and some easy, but thats expected.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Covers material too fast and his tests werent representative of the material he covered.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Cool guy, seems like he has a lot of fun in the class.  Uses a curved grading scale based on the class average.  Programming assignments can be challenging, but you should expect that anyway.  Definitely take Arup for every class possible!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: So, this is my second class with Arup, and he is a pretty good instructor. Dumbs the lectures down enough for people like me without a strong CS background- explains everything in (sometimes excruciating) detail. Love his style of teaching,and would definitely take his class again.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Good guy, drank with at a tailgating event (gave me free beer), test are hard, and tricky. Dont try to share work, really smart guy he will catch you.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Nice guy. Fun class. gives money to volunteers. exams aren't too bad but need to reason over questions a couple times, he sometimes likes to trick you. overall great professor

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Awesome guy. Very funny and provides detailed, thorough examples of concepts. I have no prior experience with programming and, though I find the class very challenging, it is nothing you can't pass with at least a C, unless you aren't trying.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: One of the best professors I've ever had.  Courses are challenging, but fair.  Really speaks clearly and at a pace that is easy to follow.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: best teacher in cs dept. as easy as cs gets, which is not that easy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Arup Guha is the bomb.  He makes programming so easy and you will learn alot.  If you already have programming background in C then don't take the class because he goes at a kind of slow pace which is good for beginners.  I def would recommend him and for any other courses!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Pretty nerdy, yet cool guy. He's very knowledable. If you go to class, he does a great job at explaining the material. His tests are fair. He "curves" your grade at the end of the semester. I think a 74ish will get u a B. This is a tough course, but he makes it really clear. I recommend him over any other programming proffesor.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Good lecture notes, but all the math on the tests got really annoying.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He get's really giddy and excited about most anything.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This is a hard class and he can be a hard teacher, but he is much better than almost all CS teachers. He cares about his students and will help you whenever you need it. Curves the tests which is great. This class is pretty hard compared to CS1 so you need to put effort into it, but he makes it easier. Got a B which isn't bad considering I'm lazy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: very like-able person, but was disappointed with him this semester. grading was bizarre with no correlation to effort and assignment value had no relation to amount of effort to complete. i enjoyed the material but did not appreciate the vagueness & lack of communication in assignments which often lead to poor grades  due to his pass/fail mentality

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This class was a lot of work, but you learn the material.  I finished with an A, but I still feel his grading scale is lazy and unfair.  Everything is graded relatively which does not allow everyone to pass with As.  Over half of the class received Cs.  The assignments take a lot of time, but are really helpful.  Do not bother buying the book.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup for the win! Unlike Intro. to C Programming, you will probably want to have the book for Intro. to OO, because the exams contain material which forces you to reference the API, unless you are extremely familiar with it already. The exams are fairly difficult, but as usual, Arup adjusts the grading scale to match the exams' difficulty.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Possibly the best CS professor in UCF. Writes helpful notes, great speaker, and does grades based on class average and SDs.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: The best math teacher I've ever had. Thought his class was impossible on the first day. Turned out to be much better. Hard class, make sure to review notes online, Tests are hard. Thank God the class is curved. Careful on quizzes. GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS. If you don't go to class, you WILL fail.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very clear lectures and sounded genuinely interested in the content.  Homework was done in groups of 2 and fairly difficult.  Final exam had some curveball questions, but other two exams were pretty good.  Overall, an enjoyable class and grade was based on mean/median of the class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great teacher, hard tests, but the grading scale is adjusted to make up for it.  Uses examples you can actually understand and relate to.  Definitely take him over the other two, you'll actually learn!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He is a great teacher.  Both him and his father are two of the best CS teachers at our school.   Take their classes if you have a chance.   Very fair teachers.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Arup is a great teacher! This is a class where you will actually learn! Very Hard Tests But Huge Curve at the end!! This guy is great!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This is my second time with Arup, and once again he did fairly well. Most of the time he was spot on, but ocassionally there were flubs or he would outright say he doesn't know this part of java very well. Regardless he tries his best, and will not test you on something he didn't teach well. Grades are VERY curved, lenient guy. He was usually late.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: EXCELLENT teacher! one of my favorite at ucf. the notes are posted online but going to class makes it so much easier to understand. exams are fairly hard and the programs are challenging but fun. i suggest him 100% if possible.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup was always my favorite CS teacher but I dont recommend him for CS3. Covers material too fast and his tests werent representative of the material he covered. 40% of the first test was based on solving 2 rather tricky programming problems that werent discussed in class at all. Hes still a good teacher and I recommend him for anything below CS3.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This guy is such a great professor.  The first bunch of programming assignments are easy, the last few are kind of hard, but he grades really easy (something like a 45% in the class is a C) The MC parts of his tests are really tricky, but he gives out a free answer with the last question everytime Free reponse is a piece of cake. Will take him agai

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great guy, great teacher.  Highly recommended.  I don't think I would have ever liked the subject so much but for his teaching.  Many students find his exams and assignments hard, but he will help you understand the material.  C programming can be challenging and confusing to some of us, and this is the best teacher to learn from.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very nice guy. Takes a lot to get an A in the course though as his exams are very challenging, but he's a nice guy. Do his work, show up to class (he takes attendance) and do the homework and you should be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup is a fantastic teacher! His tests and homework assignments are challenging, but you learn so much from them. He also has tremendous respect and concern for his students. He isn't out to fail students, so it is easy to pass if you pay attention.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Really good teacher, I cannot stress enough take this professor. He is a really nice guy and acutally teaches the material in a method so you can understand it, also goes over tests and programming assignments and what was suppose to be done on them. Seriously take him you will have a great time.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4810 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: DO NOT take him for topics!!!! Usually there are two sections of this course.  Guha's course requires WAY more work then either Orooji or Leeson. Try a different section.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Arup has been both my favorite and my most difficult professor. You never really grasped the concepts until doing the assignments, but there were not a lot of assignments at all (however, I wouldn't ask for more!). Arup was VERY helpful during office hours, and I would definitely recommend him (he speaks english.)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: hes a cool teacher and likes to do a lot of beer examples lol. the material is covered completely so if u pay attention you should be ok. the only problem i had is the learning environment was horrible plus the lack of assignments made grasping the material difficult. but, hes a good teacher so id recommend him over others.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Insanely hard, but worth it.  I usually like to skate by with minimal effort whether I learn anything or not.  With Arup its a different story.  He challenges you and is very fair about it.  Always willing to help.  Makes CS interesting.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very good teacher.  Knows what he's talking about, and can handle almost any question thrown at him.  Those with previous programming experience might be a tad bored, but he covers all the material very thoroughly.  Grades are kind of sparse, but he curves (grading philosophy is on his website).  Definitely a good professor to choose if available.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Great professor. It's Comp Sci, so don't expect an easy class, but he makes learning worth it. Often explains topics so they are easy to understand. A lot of programming homework, but in pairs (two people).  As always, do the work and you'll be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Probably the coolest professor I've ever met. He's a good teacher, a really smart guy, and real laid back. He projects well to the class as well.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I wish I had Arup for CS1 and CS2, because I learned the most from his lectures and assignments in CS3.  Tests are exceptionally hard, but he grades extremely fair (a 60 may equate to a B).  All around great instructor.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Arup Guha is one of the best instructors at UCF.  His class is challenging, but he more concerned with your learning of the information than the grades.  He is a fair teacher, who is easy to approach and is willing to help as long as your willing to help yourself as well.  He is one of the only instructors that has not pushed me to a TA for aid.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: IT majors will have a very hard time with him. Unless of course you are getting a minor or already have a BS degree in mathematics.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: good notes, basically gives you the code for programming assignments beforehand. tests are hard, but liberal curve is always applied, usually A>=80.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Disrespectful and immature.  His teaching does nothing to prepare you for the tests he gives.  Stay away from this guy.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: really helpful.If you don't miss any class and go over the notes at home, you will get an A for sure.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Don't take his classes
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Biased and judgemental. Avoid if you can.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP35023503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Guha is the best programming teacher at UCF. Some of the veteran professors could learn a thing or two from him. Guha's test are hard, but he genuinely cares for all of his students. He is always available in his office, and he will help you out grade wise as long as he sees that you are trying. Take him for any CS classes that you can.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Somtimes the notes were not completely clear, and more was added to them during the class so make sure to take notes.  The tests were hard, and although a study sheet is allowed, it doesn't really help.  Class is curved at the end.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: horrible professor...if you dont get it his way then you are SOL.  I think he is the most unfair professor that I have ever had at UCF.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very very helpful, very clear.  Gives tons of examples that he mostly comes up with himself.  I had no previous programming knowledge and got an A-.  Grades according to class performance, not material.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This was my second class with him, a very easy professor to understand, but kind of hard class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Very good professor.  You will be bored stiff if you already know the material, but he really knows how to break it down for everyone else.  He will always stop what he is doing during office hours for you and he and his TA's always answer emails.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: The material isn't easy, but he does a good job in going over it. I had no computer programming experience beforehand and got a B. The beginning of the class is very easy and the last two or three weeks are hard. Be sure to do well on the tests so you dont have to worry about the last 2 assignments.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Dont listen to these uneducated fools who say he is hard.  He is only hard if you cannot program at all and have no place being in CS to begin with.  He is one of the best CS teachers at UCF and I would highly recommend him to anyone.  Tests are hard but fair, as well as the programming assignments.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This was my second class with Arup, and he didn't dissappoint what I had come to know and love about his teaching style.  He's expert at explaining tough concepts, and you've gotta love a teacher that WANTS you to call them by their first name.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He makes great lecture notes that he posts online, stresses the important concepts in class, and gives ample examples that are often amusing. Tests are tricky, but as long as you do the sample ones he provides, you know what is coming. He is very helpful if you ask. Great instructor!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Even though my class was only once a week for three hours he managed to provide an ample amount of samples for his assignments. His grading policy is one that I'd recommend to all the other teachers.He did put a few problems on one of his tests before he taught the material but overall a great teach
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not_Sure_Yet | Review: A very bad professor.  His tests were a nightmare and well beyond what was taught in class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I also had him for Intro to C and i work for him.  Hes a really nice guy and really helpfull.  And you can understand him, and as most CS majors know that its hard to find a professor that you can understand.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: This is my first programming class, so having no previous knowlege, I was a bit worried.  Guha made everything very easy to understand, and made even the most complex topics seem easy.  Tests may seem tricky, but just look over sample tests he gives you before the real thing. Grading policy nice too

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I felt he was a horrible teacher and that his tests were unfair.  I would never take him again and think everyone should avoid him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Amazing teacher, he has a way of explaining complex topics in a manner that allows you to understand it the 1st time you hear it and most importantly allows you to remember it. I didn't buy the book, never studied, and got an A both class I had him for.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: He is very easy to understand and talk to.  Assignments are usually short.  Need to come to class as much as possible, even if you know C!!!!  Gives make-up exams/quizzes without much question.  If you found the class hard, you prolly skipped alot.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He gives extremely difficult programs and tests.  You can expect all kinds of curves and tricks with him.  If you dont understand it exactly as he intends it, you get nowhere.  I know several people who have changed majors because of him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very good teacher, covers the material very well and speaks clearly. He knows what he is talking about.  His tests are very difficult, a lot of unexpected twists on the test.  Programs are a little bit hard but he gives you enough time to do them.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: His lectures are very clear and structured.  He's a really good teacher but his tests are incredibly tough!  He uses his own grading scale (determined at the end of the semester).

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Speaks clearly, uses a lot of examples, programs are a tad over-board. Don't this course along with another programming course.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Awesome CS1 Teacher, very smart, and actually explains the material very well. Class is tough, but if you study you should be good.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Most helpful/explanatory programming teacher I've ever had. Grades on tests/programs may be bad, but he curves accordingly and generously. Really helps students understand material. I will take more courses with him if at all possible. I recommend!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Extremely nice and helpful, he may be the best in the CS department...at the same time, his tests are very difficult and are based almost completley on reading trick questions. Huge curve tho.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: You may think you have an understanding of the material, but he throws massive curves on the tests which leave you asking where he got this.  If you are willing, learn with another professor and dont waste your time.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Excellent teacher, but can be hard on tests.  Arup teaches a lot more about C and tries to make sure that the whole class understands what he teaches that day.  However, if you're not willing to learn, you shouldn't bother.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: if you dont get it his way, you wont get any credit on the tests.  Think like him or else!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: unreasonably hard
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: VARIOUSCS | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is not only one of the best CS teachers I have had, he is also one of the coolest. He pushes students to work harder but also explains concepts fully using concepts that we already understand. Great guy, I take as many classes from him as I can! :)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: class is the hard no doubt but he is good at what he does but if its ur first programming class you may want to save your GPA now but if you do take it study study study and take advantage of all of the TA's office hours they were the only reason i passed

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Class is curved a lot.  If you practice, you will do well.  If you know C, you don't have to go to class :)  Just keep up with the homework, and go to the reviews, and you'll get an A

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He was great at explaining the C language. If you attend class you WILL understand how to program in C, he goes over in depth EVERY aspect of the code and techniques he expects you to know. Tests are definetly tricky but he gives you a massive curve.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He is very good at keeping such a boring class fun. He would make a good babysitter too, so many whiney kids in the class needing their noses wiped. By the way if you are a know it all about programming type, keep your mouth shut and just nod already!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Good intro to comp sci class

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is a hard tester. He is an excellent teacher. He does a superb job of explaining things and showing how things work. He makes students into real CS majors who actually know stuff instead of just get by. he spends money on food for us too! woohoo

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: VARIOUSCS | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Best teacher in the department, bar none.  Too bad you can't take all of your CS from him...you'd actually learn something

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT5405 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: great teacher ... should take more grad classes .. wish i had some one like him in my undergrad classes..makes the tough class so fun and easy to understand....
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CAP5937 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: ST: Cryptography.  Hard class but great teacher.  I never fell asleep in his class... that's very good!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: One of the top 3 CS teachers I had during my undergrad at UCF. It's hard to appriciate how good of a teacher Arup is; until you've experienced a poor teacher teaching the same subject. Really cares about teaching, and it shows. He speaks perfect english.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This is such a hard class! I had never programmed before and felt really stupid the first few weeks. He is an extrememly nice teacher and helps you out a lot though. You definately have to work hard.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: tests make your brain hurt but are curved heavily.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: His class is very difficult, but he tries to make it easier to understand.  His class notes are posted on the web and grades on a curve.  Stick with the class, but you can't be lazy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: EXTREMELY HARD!! If you haven't taken a programming class before than you are in trouble. I had to WITHDRAWAL from it after the first program and test because my grades were sooo low... If you turn anything in wrong like .doc instead of .c you get a -1!!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Had him for COP3503, COP3530, COT3100, and now COT4810.  Extremely approachable, friendly, and honestly cares about whether or not his students learn.  Brings confusing concepts down to a level that is understandable (ex. 12 permutations of the word beer)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Awesome teacher.  Puts class notes online.  Very difficult class, but he is extremely good at presenting the material.  Perfect english.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Had for cs2, cs3, and discrete.  Very good teacher.  One of the best by far.  Cool Cool guy.  Hard classes and hard tests, but he grades on class average.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very good teacher.  Always at least 5 minutes late for class, but that might just have been my class.  Tests are hard, but I haven't taken a CS course with easy tests yet.  He's good at explaning things and very helpful during office hours
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Difficult teacher but you'll leave the class knowing a lot.  The curve also helps!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: One of the few CS professors who isn't terrible.  Nice guy, hard class, nice curve.  Take this guy as much as you can.  He's the only one with personality.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Hard tests, way too smart for his own good. Very nice. Amazing curve. Very cool guy, invites all students of drinking age out for a beer after the final.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: great teacher w/ great personality and teaching style.  however, concepts are near impossible to grasp and so are the tests.  the best part is that he grades on a curve.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Very hard teacher. the tests are awful. but every one in the class stuggles so it's not as bad. Speaks perfect english so you can't make that an excuse
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: LOVE HIM!!! A little tough, but who doesn't like it that way ;)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: DISCSTRUC | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Ok, it's true that his tests are hard. But his lectures are clear and understanding so, he kind of has a right to make them hard. Great sense of humor, and loves Latitudes :)
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I agree - the tests are horrible, but the curve makes up for it.  So far, he's my favorite teacher here at UCF.  Hopefully UCF will tenure him before they lose him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great professor...a little tough, but you learn a lot.  At the end of the class, the grading scale is adjusted to be fair (since nobody can pass this guy's tests)
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: best teacher in existence... but only if you actually want to learn...
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Makes some tests impossible, but grades on an awesome curve!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: too smart for his own good.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: 51% C? Sure why not.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: All around cool guy. Challenging exams, but grades on a curve! Great teacher.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: awesome teach

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor Kamrath was a good professor. Be ready to read A LOT. There are two proctored tests with about 20ish multiple-choice questions and 5-6 short answers, and one essay, so make sure you study. He gives great feedback, and he's a fast grader as well. If you enjoy reading a lot and finding evidence, you will do well in this class.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Kamrath's class was a pleasant experience. Grading criteria and expectations were clearly stated for every assignment. I found Professor Kamrath to be a fair grader whose feedback was extensive and very helpful. I enjoyed this class and would definitely take another by Professor Kamrath.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor Kamrath really cares about his students! The readings are super interesting and his feedback is super helpful. I highly recommend this class it's a great introduction to Literature.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Great professor! A lot of reading which is expected for this type of class. Feedback is good, answers emails quickly. Participation matters in discussion posts.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Dr. Kamrath's class is exacting, be prepared to read a good amount, and write a good amount. He is understanding and although attendance is required, he makes exemptions if you provide any form of prove for an excuse. His class is not a free A, but as long as you do the work, you will receive a good grade.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3013 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Expectations were clear and well laid out from the beginning. If you did the reading you can get a good grade overall.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Mark Kamrath was an excellent teacher. He made all the students comfortable and he engaged the students in the conversation the class.   I recommend professor Dr. Mark Kamrath to everyone and you can trust him to be a fair caring and loving teacher for the students.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class requires a lot of reading so be prepared. His feedback on discussions is extremely useful, so review it carefully because it helps a lot on the midterm and final. I had a good experience and felt the course was fair.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Caring professor. Genuinely tries to engage with students in a modern way to describe profound literary texts from the past. A class for learners. Not those skating through college chasing no education and an easy 'A'. If you are hungry for knowlegde. Go get it. You will learn new ways to interpretation and respect for literature.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Kamrath provided clear direction and feedback on all assignments. I never felt confused and appreciated the structure of this course along with his humor and videos! His personal feedback on written assignments helped me to grow as a writer. Not all professors provide feedback like this, highly recommend his class.
Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: Very good at breaking text down and analyzing lines from the readings.
Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AMER3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: You should get an A just by attending lectures and completing the assignments.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: The first discussion was kind of testing the waters, but after going to office hours, he gave me pointers on how to write, that helped boost my grade. Lots of reading but it's interesting to travel back in time to different eras and read about. He answered questions/emails really fast and gave solutions and/or clear explanations. Def Recommend!!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof. Kamrath is great. There is obviously a lot of reading and writing, but he is very clear about requirements and grading criteria. He also gives extensive feedback on every written assignment. The midterm and finals can seem daunting but his study guide gives you a lot to go on, and you have access to your notes and canvas.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AMER3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Definitely take this class! He gives a decent amount of homework, but he always grades fairly and quickly with detailed line edits! This is a teacher that genuinely cares in the work and the material, as long as you stay on top of things you'll be in good hands!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: HUM3830 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: This course is based heavily on the textbook readings, which makes sense for an online course. The chosen readings convey a lot of valuable information, which made the assignments easier to complete. The teaching style is essentially a back-and-forth between reading and completing group discussion assignments.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A- | Review: He is very easy to get in touch with, but his responses can be vague. Be specific in how you ask questions, and follow up if you need. Don't expect an A. Maybe if you are a full time student with no other responsibilities, maybe you can spend every waking hour trying to meet Dr. Kamrath's standards. Otherwise, commit yourself to an A-. You'll live.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: HUM3830 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I had Dr. Kamrath's intro to digital humanities class. The class is very easy as long as you do the required readings and watch the required videos. You're basically guaranteed to pass any assignment. Also make sure to read the instructions thoroughly the guidelines aren't always clear. However he's easy to reach if you need clarification.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Engaging lectures based on the readings for the week. Gives good feedback on the assignments and papers. There are quite a few essays, but they are easy to complete if you do the readings.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: A lot of writing, but the class is very engaging and the Professor is very responsive. I also took it during summer, so that contributed to the amount of work. The Final is also super easy, since it's open book and all the answers are given to you through the professor's weekly feedback.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Overall I did enjoy the class and professor. It was a lot of reading, but I though the grades were fair and feedback was plentiful. We did have to pick a topic for the final paper before going over some of the course material that we could write about which was a bit odd and challenging.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Overall, this was a very interesting and enjoyable class! There's lots of reading, but the assignments aren't bad as long as you manage your time well. Dr. Kamrath is very quick at responding to emails, provides great feedback, and is super helpful when it comes to any difficulties. I would love to have him as a professor again.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Lots of reading, and definitely get it done. Reply more than the minimum in discussions [5+]. Make sure you follow the rubric. Reach out to him with questions and he replies fast. MLA cite everything!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: An aspect that I enjoyed about Professor Kamrath's class was his immaculate feedback and guidance in terms of moving forward and improving your grades over the semester.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Be prepared to read a lot. Take a lot of notes. In discussions when it says there is a minimum of two posts. You need to go above and beyond and do five plus posts to get full credit. Professor is a very clear grater and gives useful and meaningful feedback on everything you submit. It's easy to understand why you got the grade that you got.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Took one of Dr. Kamrath's classes online before this one.  Both classes had plentiful feedback and engaging content, 100% would take again!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Kamrath expects a good amount from his students; as the course goes on, we're expected to read more pieces of literature and answer broader questions in discussion posts or similar assignments. There are also two tests and a final paper, but outside of those, I really enjoyed his class! His lectures are interesting and he's there to help!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENL4240 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Weekly discussions and biweekly essays. The grading for DB are heavily graded for participation, make sure you reply to your classmates. Midterm and final tests, one course paper. There are lots of reading for the DB, but poems take a bulk of the reading. Professor is nice, just make sure to participate and turn everything in.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENL4240 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Does a good job at interacting with students and giving constructive feedback. Helps apply the real world to literary analysis. Would take this course again.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENL4240 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Promptly replies to emails and provides feedback when needed. Prepare to read a lot in a short period, providing a discussion post and replies by the end of the week.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENL4240 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A- | Review: He was a great teacher. Overall great grading that was tough but made sense and helped me focus in certain areas in my writing.  Resulted in me getting an A, which I feel very proud of.  I am taking him again for another course next semester!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Kamrath was an amazing professor! He is passionate about the subject matter he teaches and passing his assignments aren't difficult. There is a lot of reading in this class but that's expected and not hard at all. Show up to class because it's mandatory to pass and do your assignments because it'll definitely help you on the exams.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: There's a LOT of reading in this course, so don't take unless you're prepared to put in time outside of class. The professor gives clear grading criteria and it's not hard to succeed if you take detailed notes during lectures and want to learn. Although the exams include essay qs they're based on what he talks about so just go to the lectures.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENL4240 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I thoroughly enjoyed Professor Kamrath's Romantic Authors course. I know many students are concerned about the "odds" of receiving an A for the course. I assure you Professor Kamrath's criteria and feedback is clear enough to guide students to an A. In my years taking online courses at UCF, I have never encountered a professor that rivals him.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class is a good way to be exposed to early American texts you won't find in lower level classes and the professor is truly excited about the readings. However, if you're taking this class for an easy A, don't it's a big commitment. The class has 50-100 pages of reading and discussions/week and a midterm and final with long essay questions.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor Kamrath really wants his students to do well. It's not hard to receive a good grade in this class if you just read texts, go to class, cite the book, and do the work. He gives good feedback and even cared enough to learn each of his students names during our in person classes :) Would take him again! Recommend him if you're on the fence

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor Kamrath provides interesting texts and interactive lectures that usually only fall short thanks to lack of class participation (in my case at least.) His work load is a decent amount, and his grading can be tough, but if you put in a good effort he grades fair and well. It's not an easy A, but an interesting class and an easy high B.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENL4240 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Professor Kamrath truly wants his students to succeed and it shows in his feedback. Beware, it is a fairly reading and writing heavy course. But that is to be expected for a course of this level. I've taken two of his classes so far and am looking forward to a third!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Chill guy, pretty easy class, just cite from your textbook a lot for high grades in discussion & paper

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: HUM3830 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: You will not receive higher than a 96% on any assignment, no matter the effort, because Kamrath refuses to give an 100% period. Midterm/Final are open note with 3ish hours to complete but are 20 MCQ, 5 short answer, and an essay WITH in text citations and a thesis statement. Get ready to fight for your life for an A (instead of A- at 93 or lower).

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: HUM3830 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: This professor is tough but fair. At the beginning it takes a while to realize what he is looking for in assignments and discussion posts. When you "meet" the criteria it is like C work so make sure to exceed the requirements. Once you understand the grading his course is interesting and challenging.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: HUM3830 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: He is great professor. There is a lot of reading for each week and occasionally videos to watch with them, so be prepared to take the time needed for that. He is a tough grader and really expects you to go above and beyond to get the A grade, but on every assignment, he gives helpful feedback and is genuinely interested in helping his students.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: HUM3830 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: He's a professor who wants assignments done a specific way, but makes sure you know exactly what to do to get the best grade. It took me half the semester to get used to his grading for discussion posts, but the class was smooth sailing once I did. Even for a fully online course, he provided detailed feedback, especially for the final project.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Good Professor. Class is challenging but also rewarding. You will learn useful information in surprisingly engaging ways.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor Kamrath runs a challenging yet rewarding class. The readings are heavy but engaging, and the assignments allow you to really dive into the material. Though a tough grader, he provides great feedback to help you improve. Unstuckstudy was a lifesaver for prepping.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Since graduating, I think back on my time with UCF, and the first thing that comes to mind is taking these courses with Professor Kamrath and how much I thoroughly enjoyed the readings, and all the space I had to fall in love with reading all over again. So long as you take an interest in this work, you'll have a wonderful time in his class!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AMER3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Kamrath is kinda tough, but fair. Weekly discussion posts and mini essays, the longer the better. Keeps up with grading with excellent personalized feedback. Provides supplemental material in the modules. Course is straight forward and well organized. I would take him again. His expectations are clearly stated, and consistent.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Dr. Kamrath challenged me this semester which I found helpful because it helped me grow as a student and a writer. The reading was heavy but interesting. The homework assignments were engaging and it allowed me to get a better understanding of the subject. The professor is available and quick to respond if you have questions.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: This class has a decent amount of reading each week, but I took it over 6 weeks, so it was accelerated. 
He was overall a great professor, provided good feedback, graded quickly, and responded to emails quickly, but his grading structure was loose. Basically said what a C would look like and then told you to do better than that if you wanted an A/B

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Prof Kamrath is going to expect you to do the readings and you are going to need to do a lot of writing.  For a Lit student this is a great class, cuz there are great readings.  Instructions are clear, so just put in the work.  You will learn a lot of good info about American Literature.  Also Prof Kamrath gives good feedback.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I throughly enjoyed the class. Be ready to write is my one piece of advice. He will give word criteria (ex: 250-300 words per question). I would suggest shooting for 200+ words over that criteria.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: He is a bit of a tough grader but he truly cares about his students and will do whatever he can to help you out and is easy to communicate with. Highly recommend if you are willing to put in the work.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: While a bit of a tough grader, his course is fair and he truly cares about his students and will do whatever he can to help you during the course. Highly recommend taking him as long as you are ready to put in the work.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: You can't go wrong with Dr. Kamrath. Just make sure to do your reading and read the assignment instructions.
Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG3817 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: With digital archives he will probably know less than you, but he does mean well.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: He offers limited extra credit, so don't rely on it to improve your grade if you're on the border (A/B or B/C). Deadlines are strict; extensions are unlikely. However, the Library Research assignment is extremely useful for learning research skills. Weekly videos complement readings for better ease while writing discussion posts.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: For an online graduate course, the major assignments were spaced out incredibly well. With that said, do not underestimate how much time goes into each assignment. Deadlines are strict; however, Dr. Kamrath is reasonably accommodating. Discussion deadlines are nonnegotiable. The research assignments were practical and had detailed instructions.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: This professor does his best to respond quickly to students' emails/responses; however, depending on the situation, he might take a few days to respond. Be ready to complete a discussion post every single week -- go above the word limit for a higher grade. Rubrics are not given. Not given much guidance with the coding assignment.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Readings are filled with practical insights and useful strategies to help enhance research abilities. Still, there is a lot to do. Quite a few textbooks required, as well as attention to detail and willingness to put theory into practice. Professor is receptive to emails & provides thorough critiques + helpful pointers for improvement.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: He corrected my grammar and assisted me with technical issues I had along the way. He provided a lot of group work which I felt was integral in terms of peer connection and networking for an online class. Overall, very practical in terms of what a person could do career-wise after earning a Master's Degree in English.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: This course seems like a lot of work and students may have trouble with balance. Still, the endpoint is rewarding and the skills acquired from this seminar will benefit future graduate work and professional development--more than one could anticipate. I found Dr. Kamrath fair; he cares about his students' realistic success. Students, be patient.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENG5009 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Took this course during the spring and it was fully online. Professor was super helpful and responsive to emails. This is a graduate course, so you are going to need to put in the work. If you work hard every week and ask for help, an A is easily attainable. I enjoyed this class :)

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: VARIOUSLIT | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: #1 thing is to keep up with readings and take notes. Mostly long paragraph response to things. Also this is for HUM3830 it wasn't in the dropdown.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Dr. Kamrath is great. The assignments consisted of group discussion posts every week and a two page paper about every other week. The class is very organized.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor Kamrath is caring and so knowledgeable if you reach out. He is open to questions and never took too long to answer my emails. If you read and put in the work that is asked of you, this is an A course.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Good lectures and weekly discussion posts, as long as you stay on top of the reading you should be good!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Dr. Kamrath's lectures are very clear and concise, and although he assigns a lot of written assignments, he always gives detailed and helpful feedback. His course requires a lot of reading, but his lessons are interesting and engaging. He's a tough grader, but a fair one. Despite the challenge of his class, Dr. Kamrath's a great professor.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3013 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Dr. Kamrath is very concise and his lectures are very easy to understand. The textbooks for the classes I've taken of his are mandatory; make sure to source your claims with quotes. Take good notes and pay attention!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: The class was easy enough to take. There are times where you are required to ready a lot of material, but the same material is explained more clearly during class.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Course was laid out well, while a lot of the readings weren't amazing in terms of being interesting and engaging. He definitely grades fairly, his lectures didn't drag on and seems genuinely interested in the subject. Reading everything assigned is not at all a requirement, he will tell you what is most important.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Professor Kamrath is a good professor. He does his best explaining the material of the course. Students don't hesitate to ask a question. Make sure to send message early. Professor will respond quickly. He provides a break down of what was learned every week. This is helpful to include in your notes for open book exams.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML4101 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I loved AML4101. It introduced me to novels I wouldn't have read. The weekly discussion posts help you analyze the texts, interact with classmates, and see how others interpret the literature. Professor Kamrath cares about his students and wants them to succeed. As long as you do the work and participate, the class isn't hard.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: This professor was excellent and he really loved the subject in which it made the class feel more like a physical class than an online one. Also, if you do a survey at the end of the semester he will bring up your grade. Make sure you study for the final exam that is the only reason why I got a B+

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I took Native American Literature with Professor Kamrath. There were tags I couldn't include, but if I could include more of them, they would be the "Participation Matters, Amazing Lectures, Gives Good Feedback and Caring". His class is reading intensive, so mentally prepare yourself for that. His video lectures and weekly feedbacks are fantastic!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: I really enjoyed Professor Kamrath's course. We had a weekly discussion due on the reading and a 2 page paper due each weekend. The paper material was always very clear and his feedback was helpful. The class was simple if you read the material and read his feedback. I highly recommend you take Professor Kamrath if you have the opportunity.
Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I would recommend this class and teacher. I would also take other classes by him! I enjoyed it!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: I had Prof. Kamrath for AML3640 Native American Lit and AML3031 American Lit I. In both classes there was a high amount of reading and writing. This should be expected.. they're college level lit courses. Dr. Kamrath gave GREAT feedback on my papers and actually read them. I can honestly say that his feedback made me a better writer.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: There's a lot of writing in this class and discussions but the way the class is set up I gained a lot from the material and comprehended it pretty well

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Good feedback on papers & grades on time. You should answer all questions thoroughly & give about 3-5 responses to other posters on substantial weekly discussion posts to get full points. Tests aren't too difficult as long as you do all the readings & posts. Heavy reading; I recommend looking 1 week ahead to start early on readings.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Great professor & fascinating course. Class is very history-focused but he gives plenty of helpful feedback every week after each discussion post (comes in handy once it's time to review for the midterm/final). Lots of interesting readings but expect to read *a lot*. Responds to emails quickly. Closely follow assignment instructions & you'll pass!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor Kamrath was genuinely a good instructor who truly dedicated his time for his students to be successful. He would reply to my messages fast and was clear with what he looked for. I feel like I actually learned something because online classes typically feel like I have to learn material myself. I recommend Professor Kamrath.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great Professor. Organized and clear guidelines. A lot of reading but engaging topics. Majority of the assignments are weekly discussion boards. The midterm/final are harder but they are open book. Make sure to read over the DB feedback each week when preparing for the exams. A few papers during the term. He gives good feedback. Would take again.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Tough grader. Knows his stuff. Get ready to read. You're taking an American literature class? You should have come ready to read. He's also a great lecturer. Sit back, pop a cold one, and get comfortable. His cadence is easy to listen to, and he's a manner of explaining that unconfounds events with clarity. He's fair. You'll earn your grade.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: VARIOUSLIT | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: I took American Lit 1, Native American Lit, American Novel, and Digital Archives with Dr. Kamrath. He uses clear grading rubrics and assigns interesting material. He was always there to help during office hours and has served as a great reference. I recommend his courses.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B- | Review: I had Professor Kamrath for American Literature. The lectures were engaging. He wants all of his students to get something out of his class. He would explain what we would be reading, and what assignment was due at the end of the week. He was even nice enough to write a letter of recommendation for me for my MFA program. Overall, a great professor.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENC5009 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Dr. Kamrath is a model professor for online classes. His Canvas is well organized. Lots of reading, bit it's purposeful, plus his assignments and grading are fair. He is personable and makes time for students if they have questions/concerns and always replies in a timely manner. Overall, he is a caring professor that I would definitely take again!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Throughout the course there is a fair amount of reading, but he was able to keep it interesting. You can tell he perseveres to keep the class upbeat. A lot of writing although thankfully grading was lenient with constructive comments.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great professor. Lectures are interesting and participation will help your grade. Can be a tough grader, but he leaves feedback and is there to help/push you improve/understand the material. Frequent readings, but nothing crazy. I would highly recommend taking the class/professor, esp. if you want a rich learning experience.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Mr. Kamrath's class has quite a lot of reading and writing assignments, I am a very good writer although I do not like to do it. However, he makes the material very interesting and provides excellent insight on what he expects. He provokes your thoughts in the right direction and cares about your understanding of the material and how to analyze it!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENC5009 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Dr. Kamrath is a difficult grader. Course is organized clearly and major assignments have comprehensive, detailed instructions.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Mixed opinions that'd I'd like to share. He's mostly fine, but there'll always be a sort of...strangely illogical wild card you have to plan around. Midterm exam: if you can't write 5 small essays, 1 big one, and 20 questions in 2 hours, GG. Final research paper...is on a week that UCF is closed? Meaning if you used Thanksgiving break...checkmate.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: If you have the option to take a different professor... you should 100% TAKE IT. I've never walked away from a conversation with him feeling heard or respected. Any concerns you have will be met with a story of how his sons both have degrees, so he "gets it".... he doesn't. Not to mention his dull teaching style. Save yourself the trouble.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Not very clear on instruction.
Excessive writing
Midterm is very long and almost absurd
Doesn't seem to know how to teach

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I have never given a rating in all four years of undergrad but I had to come here to rate so that other students might see. I'm sure the course being over six weeks impacted this, however, this was the most difficult class I have ever taken. An intense amount of readings. Excessive writing. Final exam with many irrelevant questions.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: If you need a summer A/B elective, do not take this professor, especially with any other busy courses. If you don't enjoy reading early American English, you'll be miserable for six weeks, because you may have to read a couple hundred pages and write close to 2,000 words about it in one week. If you need this course, wait till spring or fall.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Kamrath is not encouraging of different writing styles and wants his students to learn what he learns from a text. 

His feedback is based on essays he has written. Forces you to do busywork regarding minor grammatical errors, and asks you to go to outside sources for help, instead of teaching. If you are an A student, expect a B.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: The coursework is boring, but Prof. Kamrath does his best to make class engaging. You have to read and write a lot- it is a literature class. He's nice and he cares a lot about his students. I earned a C+, but it was my own fault- I missed several classes and missed a couple assignments. My biggest complaint is he leaves no feedback on discussions.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Expect to read volumes - anywhere from 100-200+ pages some weeks. Fascinating texts and videos though. Weekly discussion boards, several close-reading essay papers, monster mid-term exam, final project, final exam. Huge amount of work, but Dr. K wants you to succeed. Helpful feedback. Read/follow instructions. Do the work and you'll do well.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML4101 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: The amount of feedback given was laughable. Every week he would post a summary of how the class did as a whole, but would not give individual feedback and did not answer emails. We were assigned a book a week. Some were very complex, old English which are very hard to read in 5-7 days. Passing is manageable, but it takes a lot of self-motivation.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3013 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Mark Kamrath is an outstanding professor who truly desires his students to achieve their utmost. His course is extremely well organized and orchestrated. Dr. Kamrath takes the time to carefully grade students' work offering insightful comments for you to improve. In his class, you learn from your mistakes and improve upon your writing.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML4101 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: LOTS AND LOTS of reading! Not the best professor Ive taken but not the worst. Hes a tough grader and nitpicks at small issues. I wouldnt take him ever again.
Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: ENC5009 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Worst class and worst professor I have ever taken. Stay far away if you can.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: After three years at UCF I can honestly say that this teacher is the worst I have ever encountered. He is arrogant and unwilling to provide helpful feedback. He grades based on an unrealistic rubric. I would NOT recommend him to my worst enemy!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Only take his class In-Person!! I took his class both Online and In-Person. Failed Online, passed with an 89% In-Person. Same course material and homework with both but you get a better understanding of the material and the content he is looking for with his personal lectures than just from the readings. Pay close attention to what he says!

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: So here's the thing, Kamrath gives you A LOT of reading and writing assignments. These are weekly discussion s and several analysis papers during the course. But honestly as long as you do the readings and understand them, the papers are a breeze. Overall, it's not a difficult class, but if you don't do the needed work you're screwed.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3640 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof Kamrath really cares about his students, and this shows through interaction as well as in the way he handles your participation in the course. It's a lot of reading, and a lot of writing outside class, but worth it. He's sympathetic and genuinely wants you to succeed in and out of class. Interesting class too, clearly passionate about it.

Professor: Mark Kamrath | Course: AML3031 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR. SAVE YOUR SELF.
