She covers what she needs to and then everyone is free to go. There is a group project that makes up most of the assignments. She is very strict about participation. She followed through with her warnings for people to participate. Show up and do a little writing and you'll get an A.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Worthless class! Learned nothing! Disorganized Professor! Stupid Homework! Does not teach Java! Exams are PowerPoint slides verbatim so good luck memorizing those! Horrible! Take it with someone else or CS2 will hurt you!

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Karen is a really nice person who cares a lot about her students, but she cannot teach. She throws her students in the deep end, and then does all the swimming for them. I don't even know how to do hello world in Java. I wish the class was taught more like intro to c, step by step, building a solid foundation to structure everything else off of.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: She really wants her students to pass. Super easy class. Get ready to do group work though. She is super funny and always willing to help.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: If you need an easy A, a raise in your GPA or more credits, this is the professor for you. If you actually want to learn something, don't take her. Her online class got to take all of their tests online with notes while we didn't. She never graded and cancels class all the time. I had to teach myself everything. Doesn't answer emails but is nice

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: The class wasn't hard at all.  Professor Whiting is a pretty laid back person and realizes that this class is made up of generic facts about interfaces.  There isn't any homework really, and a UI presentation makes up most of your grade. Tests are online.  Go to class and make an A.  Don't go to class and make an A-.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CGS3175 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: This class and her inspired me to chose my career in web designing. Easy A and you still learn stuff. She is very flexible and chill oh and hilarious!Her lectures are clear cut and her slides are easy to follow. Exams are open book.  Just take her!

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Not only does she post videos to help with the assignments, but she also seems to care while making them. 10/10 would take again.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: One of the best professors I've ever had!  She cares about her students and really knows Java like she says she does. Lots of experience, and very willing to help students out in any way possible. Hilarious in class, keeps everyone entertained when she wants, and professional when she means business.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Professor Whiting is a rare type. Unlike most ucf professors, she respects you, teaches you exactly what you need to know for the class, and delivers real world examples from her many years of actual perfectional experience. Professor Whiting won't put up with your attitudes, but you will respect her for it.  You will enjoy her class. "cheers!"

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: The class was very fun. She is a cool person and doesn't make you do busy work. Everything we did in class actually helped us learn and wasn't created to add unnecessary stress.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Graded attendance, although I never felt like I really learnt anything specifically about coding from her. Powerpoints are good review for the tests, which are pretty easy (they come straight from her reviews). Assignments sometimes got hard but the TAs at the labs were very helpful. Easy A.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Also had her for Internet Applications. She's very nice and laidback, but I can't say I really learned anything in her class. She cancelled lecture multiple times and there were only 3 small projects throughout the semester. I guess it could be way harder so I shouldn't complain.
Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Talks like a sailor. Almost no work at all for the entire semester.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Warning, only take her when you are already an expert in Java or b prepared to teach yourself. If you don't know much about Java then her lecture slides won't make any sense. I had to teach myself  using outside resources. if she goes over codes step by step with students the class would make more sense.  She is very nice and pretty though.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: For COP3502, as in Computer Science 1, she made up everything as she was going along. It was as if she hadn't used C for years and was trying to remember things while reading the powerpoint slides. She unintentionally lied to the students many times, confusing C and Java very often. It was clear she has little experience with C any time recent.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Prof. Whiting is a great teacher. She teaches exactly what you need to know, tests on what she goes over in her lectures, and assigns homework based on the classwork. Coding is difficult and she is more than happy to help students be successful. She is a bit disorganized but she makes up for it with her helpfulness during her office hours.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Karin is a pretty good teacher.  She is straightforward with the content that she wants you to know, cares about her students and is also willing to spend extra time helping students.  She was a bit flaky toward the end of the semester in regard to her office hours though.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: Karin is a really nice professor, truly cares about her students.  She can be a little disorganized at times though.  It was a summer class so time got the better of us unfortunately I suppose.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Whiting is hilarious.  She really engages the students with her fascinating take on the Java programming language.  She's cut and dry, telling you what you need to know, and ONLY what you need to know.  She doesn't mess around in office hours either.  She'll point out errors in your code instantly and help you fix them.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Whiting is an alright professor. Took her for CS 1 over the summer. Some say she is an easy A but that is no longer the case, at least for her CS 1 class. I did end up with a B though. Much easier professor than Guha or Szumlanski from what I have been told, although I would have learned more if I took this course with a different professor.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Wonderful professor  who is more than fair, but not so easy that you should just expect to get an A. If you study the power point slides and the example code that she provides, you will do fine on the test. The program we built in class was fun to make. Took attendance though which did hurt my overall grade. I will definitely take her again.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor whitings is a nice professor who is willing to spend time with you for you to understand Java better. But for someone who knows very little about object oriented language and how to code, it's very difficult. All her lectures are about the functionalities of Java. She really needs to show more examples of codes even if is a summer class.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Ridiculously easy class, to the point you probably won't learn much. Be prepared to memorize her lecture slides for her tests. 23 were basically memorization tests. The third was much better, and was actually reading code. She's disorganized as all hell too. On the other hand, few professors will offer more help and she's a lovely person.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Karin Whiting goes out of her way to help her students. It's a hard subject, but she makes the class easy and enjoyable... Take advantage of her office hours.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Expect Whiting to being 15+ minutes late to lecture. Expect her to cancel lecture multiple times. Expect her to read DIRECTLY off the slides. Expect her to take attendance when she is late to class EVERY lecture. Expect group projects that are just busy work. Expect an easy A. Don't expect to learn anything. Easiest technical elective for CPEEE.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Class isn't hard, but the programs can be challenging. Google is your friend. She had a lot of personal problems during my semester, so students got uppity on her over nothing. Despite that, she extended office hours constantly, which is great because it's crowded usually. She's a very "got your back" teacher, and doesn't want to fail anyone.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Good Professor. She would help as much as she can. If you are stuck go to her office and she would guide you and get you out of the jam

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Easy A, definitely a GPA booster and looks very good. Maybe have to teach yourself a bit if you genuinely care about the material or taking the foundation exam. But having the Facebook for this class helps a lot and showed how important networking and helping others is valued in the computer science world.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor Whiting made what is traditionally considered a hard class into a very easy A. This is great if you're just looking to boast your GPA, not so much if you really want to learn CS1. However in her defense this was her first time teaching this class and she genuinely does care about her students. Overall would recommend.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This professor is just an easy A. She doesn't teach well and is lazy when it comes to preparing for lectures. She literally copies and paste from websites to create her power points. I did nothing and got an A. Way too easy. I learned nothing from her class.  Don't take if you actually want to learn.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Great teacher. Nice person. Really wants her students to do well. She will go slow and make sure you really learn the material before she moves on and will offer extra credit at the end to boost grades. We had 4 coding assignments over the course of the semester. Only critique is the class MAY not fully prepare you for foundation exam. Easy A.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Easy A, though I didn't really learn much about CS1. Her exams were multiple choice and the final was mostly recycled test questions.  A majority of the code for the assignments could be found in the slides on Webcourses. She was a very entertaining lecturer, and she gave up on the attendance sheet after a while.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Take her class for an easy A, but not to learn how to code. She teaches you how to plug stuff into pre-made templates, not how to write code from scratch. She's a nice person, though, and she's more than willing to adjust her class for the students' benefit (e.g. throwing out test questions people don't like). Don't need the textbook.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CGS3175 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Easy class, great for people who need to learn multiple languageslibraries. No tests, just online submissions of the website project as zip files. Knowledgeable teacher, but some material might be better learned by a tutor or another student. Uses W3 Schools; look it up, it's good. Book is not needed; uses powerpoints and websites.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Easy course. My first attempts at using HTML and JavaScript and I passed no problem. There is an assignment every week. She does take attendance for a grade, but I never went to the lectures. She puts up online more than enough help for the exams, if you don't pass a test you didn't bother looking at the review.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great teacher if your new to coding, like i was it isn't too bad, does required some effort on your part.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Karin Whiting is a bad professor. If you don't know object oriented programming then good luck learning OOP concepts in her class. The second half of the semester is her just running through her code for the assignments. Half of the class just pulls out their phones and records the code she has. She does not teach OOP she teaches Java GUIs.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Prof Whiting is good for beginning coders. She's funny and helpful most of the time, drops the lowest test grade and gives one extra-credit question on every exam (usually about celebrity gossip; can't answer wrong). I would take her class again, but getting help is like pulling teeth (lab is mostly worthless). The internet will be your friend.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This class is easy, I'll flat out say it. That said, Professor Whiting is pretty excellent. She's a tad disorganized and a little hectic, but I've enjoyed her class thus far. She answers questions well, polite to her students, and will go out of her way to help. Would recommend for this class. I'm not sure how she'd do with a more difficult class.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: The class is easy; too easy. The lectures are disorganized and confusing. If you already know java be prepared to be bored out of your mind.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CGS3175 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Took her for the HTML/Javascript class. She is very polite and understanding. If you have light experience with programming and HTML, this class will be easy for you. I barely showed up to class and got an A on all assignments. She tried her best to make lectures interesting, but its easy for CS/IT majors anyways.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Great teacher! I learned a lot.  She uses real world scenario to teach. If she offers another class that I need, I will definitely take her again. Assignments time consuming, tests are fairly easy.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof. Whiting was constantly readjusting her lectures to fit the needs of the students. She focused on topics that were difficult for us to grasp. Everything is posted online, so attendance is not mandatory. Don't buy the textbook. Great professor, great class, great experience. I received an A.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Very clear with assignments and instructions. Seemed to be her first semester teaching at college level. Didn't have to attend class, everything posted online.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: She is an extremely smart person. This was her first semester teaching, i thought she did a great job considering she had 3 other classes to teach. She was always willing to help others to the best of her ability. I learned a lot from the class and would definitely take this professor again.

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Super easy class, you will most likely get an A. If you are looking to learn about OOP this is not the class for you, you will not learn anything. Although she is very nice, the class was very disorganized and assignments were posted late. 4 tests (drops the lowest), assignments, short paper, and the final

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: If u in CS dont take her. She sucked at teaching & spent most of course on Java but barely on OOP concepts. This her 1st time here but I feel like I havnt learned OOP which is most important topic in CS. Her tests r easy & programs r easier since to pass u had to copy & paste existing code & change couple variables. I got 96 A & barely did anything

Professor: Karin Markle | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very nice and friendly professor. Very personal and casual lecture style. Her expectations were very clear. Plenty of time to do the class assignments. I highly recommend!
